---
title: "CCDiet_genotypes_founderProb"
author: "Dawson Budke"
date: "2025-04-23"
---

This markdown serves to to prepare the CCDiet projects genotype files for CC mice, namely CC001, CC019, and CC040 which are present in my AFRI project, to convert the genotypes into founder probability files. I need these CC founderProb files for a secondary comparison for my CC/CCRIX implementation of GBRS for diploid ASE. The first comparison demonstrated GBRS reconstructed diplotypes concordant \~95% for CC and \~90% CCRIX with "CC_founder_probs" which is Jax's GigaMUGA microarray genotyping files.

Code was augmented from Phoebe's ""CCDiet_qtlTestAnalysis1"

qtl2 GitHub: <https://github.com/rqtl/qtl2?tab=readme-ov-file>

qtl2 tutorial: <https://smcclatchy.github.io/mapping/>

## 1. Load Libraries

```{r}
# Clear enviroment to start fresh (if necessary)
rm(list = ls())

#  Load packages
library(reshape2)
library(reshape)
library(plyr)
library(dplyr)
library(stringr)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)
library(enrichR)
library(data.table)
library(qtl2)
library(qtl2convert)
library(broman)
library(readxl)
```

## 2. Load in CCDiet CC genotypes

```{r}
# Given were getting ~95% concordance w CC base strains, and ~90% concordance w CCRIX, comparing GBRS ASE vs "CC_diplotype_probs", which is pretty good, we want to see if using newer CC genotypes (microarray derived still) from our own labs genotyping works even better 

# Set your working directory to wherever the zip file is located
setwd("./Data/CCDiet")

# Function to unzip and explore a single zip file
explore_zip <- function(zip_file, extract_dir) {
  # Create the extraction directory if it doesn't exist
  if (!dir.exists(extract_dir)) {
    dir.create(extract_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  }
  
  # Unzip the file
  cat("Unzipping", zip_file, "to", extract_dir, "\n")
  unzip(zip_file, exdir = extract_dir)
  
  # List the contents of the extracted directory
  dir_contents <- list.files(extract_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  cat("First 20 files/directories in", extract_dir, ":\n")
  print(head(dir_contents, 20))
  
  # Check if the Data/CCDiet directories exist
  data_dir <- file.path(extract_dir, "Data")
  ccdict_dir <- file.path(extract_dir, "Data/CCDiet")
  
  if(dir.exists(data_dir)) {
    cat("\nData directory exists in", extract_dir, "\n")
    data_contents <- list.files(data_dir, recursive = FALSE)
    print(data_contents)
  } else {
    cat("\nData directory does not exist in", extract_dir, "\n")
  }
  
  if(dir.exists(ccdict_dir)) {
    cat("\nCCDiet directory exists in", extract_dir, "\n")
    ccdict_contents <- list.files(ccdict_dir, recursive = FALSE)
    print(ccdict_contents)
    
    # Get file information for the first few files in CCDiet
    if(length(ccdict_contents) > 0) {
      for(i in 1:min(5, length(ccdict_contents))) {
        file_path <- file.path(ccdict_dir, ccdict_contents[i])
        if(file.exists(file_path)) {
          file_info <- file.info(file_path)
          cat("File:", ccdict_contents[i], "\n")
          cat("Size:", file_info$size, "bytes\n")
          
          # Try to read the first few lines if it's a text file
          tryCatch({
            lines <- readLines(file_path, n = 5)
            cat("First few lines:\n")
            print(lines)
          }, error = function(e) {
            cat("Not a text file or error reading file:", e$message, "\n")
          })
          cat("\n")
        }
      }
    }
  } else {
    cat("\nCCDiet directory does not exist in", extract_dir, "\n")
  }
  
  # Find and unzip inner zip files
  inner_zips <- list.files(extract_dir, pattern = "\\.zip$", recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
  cat("\nFound", length(inner_zips), "inner ZIP files in", extract_dir, "\n")
  
  if(length(inner_zips) > 0) {
    for(inner_zip in inner_zips) {
      # Create directory name based on the zip file name (without extension)
      inner_dir_name <- tools::file_path_sans_ext(basename(inner_zip))
      inner_extract_dir <- file.path(extract_dir, "extracted", inner_dir_name)
      
      # Create the extraction directory if it doesn't exist
      if (!dir.exists(inner_extract_dir)) {
        dir.create(inner_extract_dir, recursive = TRUE)
      }
      
      # Unzip the inner file
      cat("Unzipping inner file:", inner_zip, "to", inner_extract_dir, "\n")
      tryCatch({
        unzip(inner_zip, exdir = inner_extract_dir)
        
        # List contents of the inner extracted directory
        inner_contents <- list.files(inner_extract_dir, recursive = FALSE)
        cat("Contents of", inner_dir_name, ":\n")
        print(head(inner_contents, 10))
        if(length(inner_contents) > 10) {
          cat("... and", length(inner_contents) - 10, "more files\n")
        }
      }, error = function(e) {
        cat("Error unzipping", inner_zip, ":", e$message, "\n")
      })
    }
  }
  
  # Function to explore file types in the extracted directory
  explore_file_types <- function(dir_path) {
    all_files <- list.files(dir_path, recursive = TRUE, full.names = TRUE)
    file_extensions <- tools::file_ext(all_files)
    extension_counts <- table(file_extensions)
    return(extension_counts)
  }
  
  # Get file type information
  file_types <- explore_file_types(extract_dir)
  cat("\nFile types in", extract_dir, ":\n")
  print(file_types)
  
  return(extract_dir)
}

# Define the zip files and extraction directories
zip_files <- c(
  "USDA_Bennett_MURGIGV01_20180301.zip", 
  "USDA_Bennett_MURGIGV01_20181129.zip"
)

extract_dirs <- c(
  "USDA_Bennett_Data_20180301",
  "USDA_Bennett_Data_20181129"
)

# Process each zip file
extracted_dirs <- list()
for (i in 1:length(zip_files)) {
  cat("\n\n======== Processing", zip_files[i], "========\n\n")
  extracted_dirs[[i]] <- explore_zip(zip_files[i], extract_dirs[i])
}

# Compare the contents of both directories to see what's different
if (length(extracted_dirs) == 2) {
  cat("\n\n======== Comparing both datasets ========\n\n")
  
  # Compare file counts
  files1 <- list.files(extracted_dirs[[1]], recursive = TRUE)
  files2 <- list.files(extracted_dirs[[2]], recursive = TRUE)
  
  cat("Number of files in", extracted_dirs[[1]], ":", length(files1), "\n")
  cat("Number of files in", extracted_dirs[[2]], ":", length(files2), "\n")
  
  # Find unique files in each directory
  unique_files1 <- setdiff(files1, files2)
  unique_files2 <- setdiff(files2, files1)
  
  cat("\nFiles unique to", extracted_dirs[[1]], ":", length(unique_files1), "\n")
  if (length(unique_files1) > 0) {
    print(head(unique_files1, 10))  # Show first 10 unique files
    if (length(unique_files1) > 10) cat("... and", length(unique_files1) - 10, "more\n")
  }
  
  cat("\nFiles unique to", extracted_dirs[[2]], ":", length(unique_files2), "\n")
  if (length(unique_files2) > 0) {
    print(head(unique_files2, 10))  # Show first 10 unique files
    if (length(unique_files2) > 10) cat("... and", length(unique_files2) - 10, "more\n")
  }
  
  # Compare common files (if they exist)
  common_files <- intersect(files1, files2)
  cat("\nNumber of common files:", length(common_files), "\n")
  
  # Check for CCDiet data specifically in both extractions
  ccdict_dir1 <- file.path(extracted_dirs[[1]], "Data/CCDiet")
  ccdict_dir2 <- file.path(extracted_dirs[[2]], "Data/CCDiet")
  
  if(dir.exists(ccdict_dir1) && dir.exists(ccdict_dir2)) {
    cat("\nComparing CCDiet directories:\n")
    
    ccdict_files1 <- list.files(ccdict_dir1, recursive = TRUE)
    ccdict_files2 <- list.files(ccdict_dir2, recursive = TRUE)
    
    cat("CCDiet files in", extracted_dirs[[1]], ":", length(ccdict_files1), "\n")
    cat("CCDiet files in", extracted_dirs[[2]], ":", length(ccdict_files2), "\n")
    
    unique_ccdict1 <- setdiff(ccdict_files1, ccdict_files2)
    unique_ccdict2 <- setdiff(ccdict_files2, ccdict_files1)
    
    cat("\nCCDiet files unique to", extracted_dirs[[1]], ":", length(unique_ccdict1), "\n")
    if (length(unique_ccdict1) > 0) print(unique_ccdict1)
    
    cat("\nCCDiet files unique to", extracted_dirs[[2]], ":", length(unique_ccdict2), "\n")
    if (length(unique_ccdict2) > 0) print(unique_ccdict2)
  }
  
  # Compare the inner extracted directories
  inner_extract_dir1 <- file.path(extracted_dirs[[1]], "extracted")
  inner_extract_dir2 <- file.path(extracted_dirs[[2]], "extracted")
  
  if(dir.exists(inner_extract_dir1) && dir.exists(inner_extract_dir2)) {
    cat("\n\n======== Comparing inner extracted directories ========\n\n")
    
    inner_files1 <- list.files(inner_extract_dir1, recursive = TRUE)
    inner_files2 <- list.files(inner_extract_dir2, recursive = TRUE)
    
    cat("Number of files in inner extracted directory of", extracted_dirs[[1]], ":", length(inner_files1), "\n")
    cat("Number of files in inner extracted directory of", extracted_dirs[[2]], ":", length(inner_files2), "\n")
    
    # Find unique files in each inner directory
    inner_unique_files1 <- setdiff(inner_files1, inner_files2)
    inner_unique_files2 <- setdiff(inner_files2, inner_files1)
    
    cat("\nFiles unique to inner extracted directory of", extracted_dirs[[1]], ":", length(inner_unique_files1), "\n")
    if (length(inner_unique_files1) > 0) {
      print(head(inner_unique_files1, 10))  # Show first 10 unique files
      if (length(inner_unique_files1) > 10) cat("... and", length(inner_unique_files1) - 10, "more\n")
    }
    
    cat("\nFiles unique to inner extracted directory of", extracted_dirs[[2]], ":", length(inner_unique_files2), "\n")
    if (length(inner_unique_files2) > 0) {
      print(head(inner_unique_files2, 10))  # Show first 10 unique files
      if (length(inner_unique_files2) > 10) cat("... and", length(inner_unique_files2) - 10, "more\n")
    }
    
    # Compare common files in inner directories
    inner_common_files <- intersect(inner_files1, inner_files2)
    cat("\nNumber of common files in inner extracted directories:", length(inner_common_files), "\n")
  }
}
```

```{r}
# Extracting just 3 CCDiet samples of interest
# CC001 = CC-276
# CC019 = CC-309
# CC040 = CC-284

## "301.zip"
# Path to your file
#file_path <- "./Data/CCDiet/USDA_Bennett_Data_20180301/extracted/USDA_Bennett_MURGIGV01_20180301_FinalReport/USDA_Bennett_MURGIGV01_20180301_FinalReport.txt"

# First, get the header information
con <- file(file_path, "r")
header_lines <- readLines(con, n = 100)
close(con)

# Find where data section starts
data_line_index <- which(header_lines == "[Data]")
header_row <- header_lines[data_line_index + 1]
headers <- strsplit(header_row, "\t")[[1]]

# Skip rows before the actual data
skip_rows <- data_line_index + 1

# Define the samples we want to extract
target_samples <- c("CC-276", "CC-309", "CC-284")

# Function to process the file in chunks and filter for specific samples
extract_samples <- function(file_path, samples, headers, skip_lines, chunk_size = 10000) {
  con <- file(file_path, "r")
  
  # Skip header lines
  readLines(con, n = skip_lines)
  
  # Initialize empty data frame with correct column names
  result_data <- data.frame(matrix(ncol = length(headers), nrow = 0))
  colnames(result_data) <- headers
  
  # Identify the 'Sample ID' column index
  sample_id_col <- which(headers == "Sample ID")
  
  # Process the file in chunks
  chunk_num <- 0
  total_rows_processed <- 0
  total_rows_kept <- 0
  
  repeat {
    chunk_num <- chunk_num + 1
    chunk <- readLines(con, n = chunk_size)
    if (length(chunk) == 0) break  # End of file
    
    total_rows_processed <- total_rows_processed + length(chunk)
    
    # Convert this chunk to a data frame
    chunk_data <- read.delim(text = paste(chunk, collapse = "\n"), 
                           header = FALSE, 
                           col.names = headers,
                           sep = "\t", 
                           quote = "",
                           stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
    
    # Filter for our target samples
    filtered_chunk <- chunk_data[chunk_data[, sample_id_col] %in% samples, ]
    
    # Append to our result if we found matching rows
    if (nrow(filtered_chunk) > 0) {
      result_data <- rbind(result_data, filtered_chunk)
      total_rows_kept <- total_rows_kept + nrow(filtered_chunk)
    }
    
    # Print progress
    cat("Processed chunk", chunk_num, "with", length(chunk), "rows. Found", 
        nrow(filtered_chunk), "matching rows. Total kept:", total_rows_kept, "\n")
    
    # Print current sample counts periodically (every 10 chunks)
    if (chunk_num %% 10 == 0) {
      cat("Current sample counts:", "\n")
      print(table(result_data[, sample_id_col]))
    }
  }
  
  close(con)
  
  cat("\nSummary:\n")
  cat("Total rows processed:", total_rows_processed, "\n")
  cat("Total rows kept:", total_rows_kept, "\n")
  cat("Sample distribution:\n")
  print(table(result_data[, sample_id_col]))
  
  return(result_data)
}

# Extract data for our target samples
system.time({
  final_report_samples <- extract_samples(file_path, target_samples, headers, skip_rows)
})

# Check the dimensions of the result
dim(final_report_samples)

# Check first few rows
head(final_report_samples)

# Save the extracted data to a file for future use
write.csv(final_report_samples, "Data/CCDiet/extracted_samples.csv", row.names = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# Check out the other files

# Read the file skipping the first two lines
LocusSummary <- read.csv("./Data/CCDiet/USDA_Bennett_Data_20180301/extracted/USDA_Bennett_MURGIGV01_20180301_LocusSummary/USDA_Bennett_MURGIGV01_20180301_LocusSummary.csv", 
                         skip = 2)

LocusXDNA <- read.csv("./Data/CCDiet/USDA_Bennett_Data_20180301/extracted/USDA_Bennett_MURGIGV01_20180301_LocusXDNA/USDA_Bennett_MURGIGV01_20180301_LocusXDNA.csv", 
                         skip = 2)

# dont think I need these, the "final_report_samples" is the df I need -- thats the input into qtl2 

# My 3 samples are only in the "301.zip" file, so removed the other one 
```

## 3. Reference Scripts

```{r}
# Phoebes "convert_cc_TestData.R"

# download and convert the Collaborative Cross data
#
# supplemental data for Srivastava et al. (2017) Genomes of the Mouse
# Collaborative Cross. Genetics 206:537-556, doi:10.1534/genetics.116.198838
#
# available at Zenodo, doi:10.5281/zenodo.377036

# required libraries
library(data.table)
library(qtl2)
library(qtl2convert)
library(broman)
library(readxl)
set.seed(83763628)

# set working directory
setwd("/Users/phoebosky/Documents/Bennett_Lab/qtl2_Analysis/CCDiet_qtl/CCDiet_qtlTest/cc_test")

# create RawData/ if not available
rawdata_dir <- "../RawData"
if(!dir.exists(rawdata_dir)) {
  dir.create(rawdata_dir)
}



# download Prob36.zip if not available
zenodo_url <- "https://zenodo.org/record/377036/files/"
zenodo_postpend <- "?download=1"
prob_file <- "Prob36.zip"
distant_file <- paste0(zenodo_url, prob_file, zenodo_postpend)
local_file <- file.path(rawdata_dir, prob_file)
if(!file.exists(local_file)) {
  message("Downloading Prob36.zip")
  download.file(distant_file, local_file)
}

# create directory for data if it doesn't exist
prob_dir <- file.path(rawdata_dir, "Prob36")
if(!dir.exists(prob_dir)) {
  dir.create(prob_dir)
}

# unzip if it hasn't been unzipped
gzfile <- file.path(prob_dir, "CC001-Uncb38V01.csv.gz")
csvfile <- file.path(prob_dir, "CC001-Uncb38V01.csv")
if(!file.exists(gzfile) && !file.exists(csvfile)) {
  message("unzipping Prob36.zip")
  unzip(local_file, exdir=prob_dir)
}

# gunzip all of the Prob36 files
if(!file.exists(csvfile)) {
  files <- list.files(prob_dir, pattern=".csv.gz$")
  
  message("unzipping the probability files")
  
  for(file in files) {
    system(paste("gunzip", file.path(prob_dir, file)))
  }
}

# load the Prob36 files and determine X, Y, and M genotypes
message("reading the probability files")
files <- list.files(prob_dir, pattern=".csv$")
strains <- sub("\\.csv$", "", files)
probs <- setNames(vector("list", length(strains)), strains)
for(i in seq_along(files)) {
  probs[[i]] <- data.table::fread(file.path(prob_dir, files[i]), data.table=FALSE)
}

##############################
# guess the rest of the cross order
##############################
message("inferring cross order")
mprob <- t(sapply(probs, function(a) colMeans(a[a[,2]=="M", paste0(LETTERS, LETTERS)[1:8]])))
yprob <- t(sapply(probs, function(a) colMeans(a[a[,2]=="Y", paste0(LETTERS, LETTERS)[1:8]])))
xprob <- t(sapply(probs, function(a) colMeans(a[a[,2]=="X", paste0(LETTERS, LETTERS)[1:8]])))
# a bunch where we can't tell Y or M

# also need the supplementary data file
supp_file <- "SupplementalData.zip"
distant_file <- paste0(zenodo_url, supp_file, zenodo_postpend)
local_file <- file.path(rawdata_dir, supp_file)
if(!file.exists(local_file)) {
  message("downloading SupplementalData.zip")
  download.file(distant_file, local_file)
}

# extract just the CCStrains.csv file
csv_file <- "SupplmentalData/CCStrains.csv"
if(!file.exists(csv_file)) {
  message("unzipping SupplementalData.zip")
  unzip(local_file, csv_file, exdir=rawdata_dir)
}
csv_file <- file.path(rawdata_dir, csv_file)
ccstrains <- data.table::fread(csv_file, data.table=FALSE)

# check that the strain names are the same
stopifnot( all( paste0(sub("/", "-", ccstrains$Strain, fixed=TRUE), "b38V01") ==
                  strains))

# download table S2
url <- "http://www.genetics.org/highwire/filestream/438137/field_highwire_adjunct_files/10/TableS2.xlsx"
file <- basename(url)
local_file <- file.path("..", "RawData", file)
if(!file.exists(local_file)) {
  download.file(url, local_file)
}
tab <- as.data.frame(readxl::read_xlsx(local_file))

# check that the strain names are the same
stopifnot( all( paste0(sub("/", "-", tab$Strain, fixed=TRUE), "b38V01") ==
                  strains))

funnel <- tab[,"Funnel Code"]
mtdna <- tab[,"Mitochondria"]
ychr <- tab[,"Chromosome Y"]

# determine cross orders; use orders in Table S2 when available
cross_info <- matrix(ncol=8, nrow=length(strains))
dimnames(cross_info) <- list(ccstrains$Strain, LETTERS[1:8])
for(i in which(!is.na(funnel))) {
  cross_info[i,] <- match(unlist(strsplit(funnel[i], "")), LETTERS)
}

# when not available, use the values in table S2
use_mtdna <- mtdna; use_mtdna[mtdna=="A/D" | mtdna=="D/A"] <- "A" # use A when A/D or D/A
cross_info[is.na(cross_info[,1]),1] <- match(use_mtdna, LETTERS[1:8])[is.na(cross_info[,1])]
cross_info[is.na(cross_info[,8]),8] <- match(ychr, LETTERS[1:8])[is.na(cross_info[,8])]

# problems: CC013, CC023, CC027
# CC013 M = Y = E [genotypes say Y could be B or C, too] --- change Y to B

cross_info["CC013/GeniUnc", 8] <- 2

# check cases where mitochondrial genotype is clear
max_mprob <- apply(mprob, 1, max)
wh_mprob <- apply(mprob, 1, which.max)
stopifnot( all(cross_info[max_mprob>0.9,1] == wh_mprob[max_mprob > 0.9]) )

stopifnot( all( cross_info[,1] != cross_info[,8] ))

# are there any obvious differences?

# mitochondria
stopifnot( all( sort(max_mprob[wh_mprob != cross_info[,1]]) < 0.5))

# now the Y chr
max_yprob <- apply(yprob, 1, max)
wh_yprob <- apply(yprob, 1, which.max)
stopifnot( all( sort(max_yprob[wh_yprob != cross_info[,8]]) < 0.5))

# founders with largest X chr probabilities
wh_xprob <- t(apply(xprob, 1, order, decreasing=TRUE))

# most common X chr genotype that's not the mtDNA one in the 3rd slot
cross_info[is.na(cross_info[,3]),3] <- wh_xprob[is.na(cross_info[,3]),1]
stopifnot( all(cross_info[,1] != cross_info[,3]) )

# sort other X chr probs...put three most probable in the 2nd, 5th, and 6th slots
for(i in which(is.na(cross_info[,2]))) {
  z <- (wh_xprob[i, ] %wnin% cross_info[i, c(1,3,8)])
  cross_info[i, c(2,5,6)] <- sample(z[1:3])
  cross_info[i, c(4,7)] <- sample(z[4:5])
}

# further problems:
# CC031 Y chr is B but this is clearly on the X chromosome
# CC037 Y chr is D but this is clearly on the X chromosome
# CC056 Y chr is E but this is clearly on the X chromosome
# ... swap these with one of 2,5,6

cross_info["CC031/GeniUnc", ] <- c(1,6,5,3,7,2,4,8)
cross_info["CC037/TauUnc", ] <- c(3,4,8,7,6,1,5,2)
cross_info["CC056/GeniUnc", ] <- c(3,8,1,7,5,4,6,2)

stopifnot( all(apply(cross_info, 1, sort) == 1:8) )

message("writing cross and covariate data")

# write the cross information to a file
write2csv(cbind(id=rownames(cross_info), cross_info),
          "../cc_crossinfo.csv",
          comment=paste("Cross information for Collaborative Cross (CC) lines inferred from",
                        "genotypes from Srivastava et al. (2017)",
                        "doi:10.1534/genetics.116.198838,",
                        "data at doi:10.5281/zenodo.377036"),
          overwrite=TRUE)


# write covariate info with M and Y as inferred
covar <- data.frame(id=rownames(cross_info),
                    mitochondria=tab$Mitochondria,
                    Ychr=tab[,"Chromosome Y"],
                    n_founders=tab[,"# of Founders"],
                    origin=tab[,"Origin of Strain"],
                    stringsAsFactors=FALSE)

write2csv(covar, "../cc_covar.csv",
          comment=paste("Covariate information for Collaborative Cross (CC) lines inferred from",
                        "genotypes from Srivastava et al. (2017)",
                        "doi:10.1534/genetics.116.198838,",
                        "data at doi:10.5281/zenodo.377036"),
          overwrite=TRUE)

# download genotypes.zip if not available
genotype_file <- "genotypes.zip"
distant_file <- paste0(zenodo_url, genotype_file, zenodo_postpend)
local_file <- file.path(rawdata_dir, genotype_file)
if(!file.exists(local_file)) {
  message("downloading genotypes.zip")
  download.file(distant_file, local_file)
}

# create directory for data if it doesn't exist
genotype_dir <- file.path(rawdata_dir, "genotypes")
if(!dir.exists(genotype_dir)) {
  dir.create(genotype_dir)
}

# unzip if it hasn't been unzipped
file <- file.path(genotype_dir, "SEQgenotypes.csv")
if(!file.exists(file)) {
  message("unzipping genotypes")
  unzip(local_file, exdir=genotype_dir)
}

# read genotypes
message("reading genotypes")
g <- data.table::fread(file, data.table=FALSE)
g[g=="N" | g=="H"] <- NA


# founder genotypes from figshare
# https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.5404762.v2
fg_url <- "https://ndownloader.figshare.com/files/13623080"
local_file <- file.path(rawdata_dir, "MMnGM_processed_files.zip")
if(!file.exists(local_file)) {
  message("downloading founder genotypes")
  download.file(fg_url, local_file)
}

# unzip the founder genotype data
if(!file.exists(file.path("..", "MMnGM", "MMnGM_info.csv"))) {
  message("unzipping founder genotypes")
  unzip(local_file, exdir="..")
}

# load the allele codes
fga <- read_csv(file.path("..", "MMnGM", "MMnGM_allelecodes.csv"))
fga <- fga[fga$chr %in% c(1:19,"X"),]

# omit markers not in founder genotypes
g <- g[g$marker %in% rownames(fga),]
fga <- fga[rownames(fga) %in% g$marker,]

# reorder rows of genotype data to match fga
g <- g[match(g$marker, rownames(fga)),]

# cut down to just the genotypes
rownames(g) <- g[,1]
g <- g[,-(1:3)]

# strip off individual IDs from column names
colnames(g) <- paste0(sapply(strsplit(colnames(g), "Unc"), "[", 1), "Unc")
stopifnot( all( colnames(g) == ccstrains$Strain ))

# encode genotypes
message("encoding genotypes")
g <- encode_geno(g, fga[,c("A","B")], cores=0)

# omit markers with no data
g <- g[rowSums(g!="-") > 0, , drop=FALSE]

# write genotypes to files, one chromosome at a time
message("writing genotypes")
for(chr in c(1:19,"X")) {
  file <- paste0("../cc_geno", chr, ".csv")
  gsub <- g[rownames(g) %in% rownames(fga)[fga$chr==chr], , drop=FALSE]
  write2csv(cbind(marker=rownames(gsub), gsub), file,
            overwrite=TRUE,
            comment=paste("Chromosome", chr, "genotypes",
                          "for Collaborative Cross (CC) lines inferred from",
                          "genotypes from Srivastava et al. (2017)",
                          "doi:10.1534/genetics.116.198838,",
                          "data at doi:10.5281/zenodo.377036"))
}


# create JSON file
chr <- c(1:19,"X")
message("Write control file")
write_control_file("../cc_test.json", crosstype="risib8",
                   geno_file=paste0("cc_geno", chr, ".csv"),
                   founder_geno_file=paste0("MMnGM/MMnGM_foundergeno", chr, ".csv"),
                   gmap_file=paste0("MMnGM/MMnGM_gmap", chr, ".csv"),
                   pmap_file=paste0("MMnGM/MMnGM_pmap", chr, ".csv"),
                   pheno_file = "CC_HPphenoTest1.csv", # This MUST be located one directory up from this script!
                   covar_file="cc_covar.csv",
                   crossinfo_file="cc_crossinfo.csv",
                   geno_codes=c("A"=1, "B"=3),
                   alleles=LETTERS[1:8],
                   xchr="X",
                   geno_transposed=TRUE,
                   founder_geno_transposed=TRUE,
                   description=paste("CCDiet_test"),
                   overwrite=TRUE)


# create zip file
message("Create zip file")
zip_datafiles("../cc_test.json", overwrite=TRUE)


# create JSON file with new phenotype df
chr <- c(1:19,"X")
message("Write control file")
write_control_file("../cc_test1.json", crosstype="risib8",
                   geno_file=paste0("cc_geno", chr, ".csv"),
                   founder_geno_file=paste0("MMnGM/MMnGM_foundergeno", chr, ".csv"),
                   gmap_file=paste0("MMnGM/MMnGM_gmap", chr, ".csv"),
                   pmap_file=paste0("MMnGM/MMnGM_pmap", chr, ".csv"),
                   pheno_file = "CC_HPphenoTest2.csv", # This MUST be located one directory up from this script!
                   covar_file="cc_covar.csv",
                   crossinfo_file="cc_crossinfo.csv",
                   geno_codes=c("A"=1, "B"=3),
                   alleles=LETTERS[1:8],
                   xchr="X",
                   geno_transposed=TRUE,
                   founder_geno_transposed=TRUE,
                   description=paste("CCDiet_test"),
                   overwrite=TRUE)

# create zip file
message("Create zip file")
zip_datafiles("../cc_test1.json", overwrite=TRUE)


# create JSON file with gene exp df (all strains)
chr <- c(1:19,"X")
message("Write control file")
write_control_file("../cc_gExpTest1.json", crosstype="risib8",
                   geno_file=paste0("cc_geno", chr, ".csv"),
                   founder_geno_file=paste0("MMnGM/MMnGM_foundergeno", chr, ".csv"),
                   gmap_file=paste0("MMnGM/MMnGM_gmap", chr, ".csv"),
                   pmap_file=paste0("MMnGM/MMnGM_pmap", chr, ".csv"),
                   pheno_file = "CC_GexpTestAll.csv", # This MUST be located one directory up from this script!
                   covar_file="cc_covar.csv",
                   crossinfo_file="cc_crossinfo.csv",
                   geno_codes=c("A"=1, "B"=3),
                   alleles=LETTERS[1:8],
                   xchr="X",
                   geno_transposed=TRUE,
                   founder_geno_transposed=TRUE,
                   description=paste("CCDiet_gExpTest"),
                   overwrite=TRUE)

# create zip file
message("Create zip file")
zip_datafiles("../cc_gExpTest1.json", overwrite=TRUE)


# create JSON file with gene exp df (3 colors)
chr <- c(1:19,"X")
message("Write control file")
write_control_file("../cc_gExpTest2.json", crosstype="risib8",
                   geno_file=paste0("cc_geno", chr, ".csv"),
                   founder_geno_file=paste0("MMnGM/MMnGM_foundergeno", chr, ".csv"),
                   gmap_file=paste0("MMnGM/MMnGM_gmap", chr, ".csv"),
                   pmap_file=paste0("MMnGM/MMnGM_pmap", chr, ".csv"),
                   pheno_file = "CC_GexpTestMini.csv", # This MUST be located one directory up from this script!
                   covar_file="cc_covar.csv",
                   crossinfo_file="cc_crossinfo.csv",
                   geno_codes=c("A"=1, "B"=3),
                   alleles=LETTERS[1:8],
                   xchr="X",
                   geno_transposed=TRUE,
                   founder_geno_transposed=TRUE,
                   description=paste("CCDiet_gExpTest"),
                   overwrite=TRUE)


# create zip file
message("Create zip file")
zip_datafiles("../cc_gExpTest2.json", overwrite=TRUE)

```

```{r}
# convert GeneSeek FinalReport files to format for R/qtl2
#
# - creates one genotype CSV file for each chromosome
#
# - also creates 2 files containing the two channels of SNP intensities for markers on the X and Y chr
#   (these are useful for verifying the sex of the mice)

# load required packages
library(data.table)
library(qtl2convert)

# file containing allele codes for GigaMUGA data
#   - from GM_processed_files.zip, https://doi.org/10.6084/m9.figshare.5404759
codefile <- "GM/GM_allelecodes.csv"

# input files with GigaMUGA genotypes
#  - can be a single file or a vector of multiple files
#  - if samples appear in multiple files, the genotypes in later files
#    will be used in place of genotypes in earlier files
#  - files can be gzipped (".gz" extension)
ifiles <- c("batch1_FinalReport.txt",
            "batch2_FinalReport.txt")

# file "stem" for output files
# output files will be like "gm4qtl2_geno19.csv"
ostem <- "gm4qtl2"

# version of data.table::fread() where data.table=FALSE is the default
myfread <- function(filename, data.table=FALSE, ...) data.table::fread(filename, data.table=data.table, ...)


# read genotype codes
codes <- myfread(codefile)

full_geno <- NULL
cXint <- cYint <- NULL

for(ifile in ifiles) {
    cat(" -File:", ifile, "\n")
    rezip <- FALSE
    if(!file.exists(ifile)) {
        cat(" -Unzipping file\n")
        system(paste("gunzip", ifile))
        rezip <- TRUE
    }

    cat(" -Reading data\n")
    g <- myfread(ifile)
    # subset to the markers in the codes object
    g <- g[g[,"SNP Name"] %in% codes[,"marker"],]

    # NOTE: may need to revise the IDs in the 2nd column
    samples <- unique(g[,"Sample ID"])

    # matrix to contain the genotypes
    geno <- matrix(nrow=nrow(codes), ncol=length(samples))
    dimnames(geno) <- list(codes[,"marker"], samples)

    # fill in matrix
    cat(" -Reorganizing data\n")
    for(i in seq(along=samples)) {
        if(i==round(i,-1)) cat(" --Sample", i, "of", length(samples), "\n")
        wh <- (g[,"Sample ID"]==samples[i])
        geno[g[wh,"SNP Name"],i] <- paste0(g[wh,"Allele1 - Forward"], g[wh,"Allele2 - Forward"])
    }

    cat(" -Encode genotypes\n")
    geno <- qtl2convert::encode_geno(geno, as.matrix(codes[,c("A","B")]))

    if(is.null(full_geno)) {
        full_geno <- geno
    } else {
        # if any columns in both, use those from second set
        full_geno <- qtl2convert::cbind_smother(full_geno, geno)
    }

    # grab X and Y intensities
    cat(" -Grab X and Y intensities\n")
    gX <- g[g[,"SNP Name"] %in% codes[codes$chr=="X","marker"],]
    gY <- g[g[,"SNP Name"] %in% codes[codes$chr=="Y","marker"],]
    cX <- matrix(nrow=sum(codes$chr=="X"),
                 ncol=length(samples))
    dimnames(cX) <- list(codes[codes$chr=="X","marker"], samples)
    cY <- matrix(nrow=sum(codes$chr=="Y"),
                 ncol=length(samples))
    dimnames(cY) <- list(codes[codes$chr=="Y","marker"], samples)
    for(i in seq(along=samples)) {
        if(i==round(i,-1)) cat(" --Sample", i, "of", length(samples), "\n")
        wh <- (gX[,"Sample ID"]==samples[i])
        cX[gX[wh,"SNP Name"],i] <- (gX$X[wh] + gX$Y[wh])/2

        wh <- (gY[,"Sample ID"]==samples[i])
        cY[gY[wh,"SNP Name"],i] <- (gY$X[wh] + gY$Y[wh])/2
    }
    if(is.null(cXint)) {
        cXint <- cX
        cYint <- cY
    } else {
        # if any columns in both, use those from second set
        cXint <- qtl2convert::cbind_smother(cXint, cX)
        cYint <- qtl2convert::cbind_smother(cYint, cY)
    }

    if(rezip) {
        cat(" -Rezipping file\n")
        system(paste("gzip", ifile))
    }
}

# write X and Y intensities
cat(" -Writing X and Y intensities\n")
qtl2convert::write2csv(cbind(marker=rownames(cXint), cXint),
                       paste0(ostem, "_chrXint.csv"),
                       paste(ostem, "X chr intensities"),
                       overwrite=TRUE)
qtl2convert::write2csv(cbind(marker=rownames(cYint), cYint),
                       paste0(ostem, "_chrYint.csv"),
                       paste(ostem, "Y chr intensities"),
                       overwrite=TRUE)

# write data to chromosome-specific files
cat(" -Writing genotypes\n")
for(chr in c(1:19,"X","Y","M")) {
    mar <- codes[codes$chr==chr,"marker"]
    g <- full_geno[mar,]
    qtl2convert::write2csv(cbind(marker=rownames(g), g),
                           paste0(ostem, "_geno", chr, ".csv"),
                           paste0(ostem, " genotypes for chr ", chr),
                           overwrite=TRUE)
}

```

## 4. Begin Pipeline

```{r}
# Load in CCDiet CC genotype for 3 CC strains
CC_genotypes = read.csv("./Data/CCDiet/extracted_samples.csv")
# GigaMUGA microarray 
```

```{r}
# Download/extract CC allele files - downloaded directory from website then Globus transferred 

# Create directories for downloaded files
rawdata_dir <- "Data/CCDiet/RawData"
gm_dir <- file.path(rawdata_dir, "GM")

if(!dir.exists(gm_dir)) {
  dir.create(gm_dir)
}

# Unzip the file if it hasn't been unzipped
gm_zip <- file.path(rawdata_dir, "GM_processed_files.zip")
gm_allele_file <- file.path(gm_dir, "GM_allelecodes.csv")

if(!file.exists(gm_allele_file)) {
  message("Unzipping GM_processed_files.zip")
  unzip(gm_zip, exdir=rawdata_dir)
}

# Verify the files exist
if(file.exists(gm_allele_file)) {
  message("Successfully extracted GM files.")
  message("Found allele codes file: ", gm_allele_file)
  
  # List the files in the GM directory
  message("Files in GM directory:")
  files <- list.files(gm_dir)
  print(files)
} else {
  message("ERROR: Failed to extract the GM files.")
}
```

```{r}
# Convert GeneSeek FinalReport to R/qtl2 format - using filtered "CC_genotypes" 

# Make sure the GM directory exists and has been extracted
rawdata_dir <- "Data/CCDiet/RawData"
gm_dir <- file.path(rawdata_dir, "GM")
codefile <- file.path(gm_dir, "GM_allelecodes.csv")

# Output file stem
ostem <- "Data/CCDiet/cc_strains"

# Read allele codes file, manually processing lines
allele_lines <- readLines(codefile)
data_lines <- allele_lines[!grepl("^#", allele_lines)]
header_line <- data_lines[1]
data_lines <- data_lines[-1]

# Create the dataframe manually
codes_data <- strsplit(data_lines, ",")
codes <- data.frame(
  marker = sapply(codes_data, function(x) x[1]),
  chr = sapply(codes_data, function(x) x[2]),
  A = sapply(codes_data, function(x) x[3]),
  B = sapply(codes_data, function(x) x[4]),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

head(codes)

# Get unique samples 
samples <- unique(CC_genotypes$Sample.ID)
cat("Processing", length(samples), "samples:", paste(samples, collapse=", "), "\n")

# Subset to markers in the codes object
CC_genotypes <- CC_genotypes[CC_genotypes$SNP.Name %in% codes$marker,]

# Create a matrix to hold genotypes
geno <- matrix(nrow=nrow(codes), ncol=length(samples))
dimnames(geno) <- list(codes$marker, samples)

# Fill in the matrix
cat("Reorganizing data\n")
for(i in seq_along(samples)) {
    cat("Processing sample", i, "of", length(samples), "\n")
    wh <- (CC_genotypes$Sample.ID == samples[i])
    # Match SNP names with codes
    for(j in which(wh)) {
        snp <- CC_genotypes$SNP.Name[j]
        if(snp %in% rownames(geno)) {
            geno[snp, i] <- paste0(
                CC_genotypes$Allele1...Forward[j], 
                CC_genotypes$Allele2...Forward[j]
            )
        }
    }
}

# Encode genotypes
cat("Encoding genotypes\n")
geno <- qtl2convert::encode_geno(geno, as.matrix(codes[,c("A","B")]))

# Extract X and Y intensities
cat("Extracting X and Y intensities\n")

# For X chromosome
xmarkers <- codes$marker[codes$chr=="X"]
cX <- matrix(nrow=length(xmarkers), ncol=length(samples))
dimnames(cX) <- list(xmarkers, samples)

# For Y chromosome
ymarkers <- codes$marker[codes$chr=="Y"]
cY <- matrix(nrow=length(ymarkers), ncol=length(samples))
dimnames(cY) <- list(ymarkers, samples)

# Fill intensity matrices
for(i in seq_along(samples)) {
    cat("Processing intensities for sample", i, "of", length(samples), "\n")
    sample_data <- CC_genotypes[CC_genotypes$Sample.ID == samples[i],]
    
    # X intensities
    for(marker in xmarkers) {
        idx <- which(sample_data$SNP.Name == marker)
        if(length(idx) > 0) {
            cX[marker, i] <- (sample_data$X[idx[1]] + sample_data$Y[idx[1]])/2
        }
    }
    
    # Y intensities
    for(marker in ymarkers) {
        idx <- which(sample_data$SNP.Name == marker)
        if(length(idx) > 0) {
            cY[marker, i] <- (sample_data$X[idx[1]] + sample_data$Y[idx[1]])/2
        }
    }
}

# Write X and Y intensities
cat("Writing X and Y intensities\n")
qtl2convert::write2csv(cbind(marker=rownames(cX), cX),
                     paste0(ostem, "_chrXint.csv"),
                     paste(ostem, "X chr intensities"),
                     overwrite=TRUE)
qtl2convert::write2csv(cbind(marker=rownames(cY), cY),
                     paste0(ostem, "_chrYint.csv"),
                     paste(ostem, "Y chr intensities"),
                     overwrite=TRUE)

# Write data to chromosome-specific files
cat("Writing genotypes\n")
for(chr in c(1:19,"X","Y","M")) {
    mar <- codes$marker[codes$chr==chr]
    g <- geno[mar,, drop=FALSE]
    
    if(nrow(g) > 0) {
        qtl2convert::write2csv(cbind(marker=rownames(g), g),
                             paste0(ostem, "_geno", chr, ".csv"),
                             paste0(ostem, " genotypes for chr ", chr),
                             overwrite=TRUE)
    } else {
        cat("No markers found for chromosome", chr, "\n")
    }
}

cat("Processing complete!\n")
```

```{r}
# Determine CC "Cross_order" and then make Control file 

library(qtl2)
library(qtl2convert)

# Set working directory and paths
rawdata_dir <- "Data/CCDiet/RawData"
output_dir <- "Data/CCDiet"
prob_dir <- file.path(rawdata_dir, "Prob36")

# Load the probability files for our specific strains
message("Reading probability files")
strain_files <- c(
  "CC001-Uncb38V01.csv",
  "CC019-TauUncb38V01.csv", 
  "CC040-TauUncb38V01.csv"
)

# Map to your strain IDs
strain_mapping <- c(
  "CC001-Uncb38V01" = "CC-276",
  "CC019-TauUncb38V01" = "CC-309", 
  "CC040-TauUncb38V01" = "CC-284"
)

strain_names <- sub("\\.csv$", "", strain_files)

# Extract probabilities for each chromosome type and calculate means
message("Calculating mean probabilities")
# Initialize empty matrices for M, Y, and X chromosomes
mprob <- matrix(0, nrow=length(strain_names), ncol=8)
rownames(mprob) <- strain_names
colnames(mprob) <- LETTERS[1:8]

yprob <- matrix(0, nrow=length(strain_names), ncol=8) 
rownames(yprob) <- strain_names
colnames(yprob) <- LETTERS[1:8]

xprob <- matrix(0, nrow=length(strain_names), ncol=8)
rownames(xprob) <- strain_names
colnames(xprob) <- LETTERS[1:8]

# Process each strain's probability file
for(i in seq_along(strain_files)) {
  file_path <- file.path(prob_dir, strain_files[i])
  if(file.exists(file_path)) {
    # Read the file
    strain_data <- data.table::fread(file_path, data.table=FALSE)
    
    # Get the founder probability columns (AA, BB, etc.)
    founder_cols <- paste0(LETTERS[1:8], LETTERS[1:8])
    
    # Process mitochondrial markers
    m_rows <- which(strain_data[,2] == "M")
    if(length(m_rows) > 0) {
      for(j in 1:8) {
        col_name <- founder_cols[j]
        if(col_name %in% colnames(strain_data)) {
          mprob[i,j] <- mean(strain_data[m_rows, col_name], na.rm=TRUE)
        }
      }
    }
    
    # Process Y chromosome markers
    y_rows <- which(strain_data[,2] == "Y")
    if(length(y_rows) > 0) {
      for(j in 1:8) {
        col_name <- founder_cols[j]
        if(col_name %in% colnames(strain_data)) {
          yprob[i,j] <- mean(strain_data[y_rows, col_name], na.rm=TRUE)
        }
      }
    }
    
    # Process X chromosome markers
    x_rows <- which(strain_data[,2] == "X")
    if(length(x_rows) > 0) {
      for(j in 1:8) {
        col_name <- founder_cols[j]
        if(col_name %in% colnames(strain_data)) {
          xprob[i,j] <- mean(strain_data[x_rows, col_name], na.rm=TRUE)
        }
      }
    }
  } else {
    message("Warning: File not found: ", file_path)
  }
}

# Initialize cross_info matrix
message("Inferring cross order")
cross_info <- matrix(NA, nrow=length(strain_names), ncol=8)
rownames(cross_info) <- strain_names
colnames(cross_info) <- LETTERS[1:8]

# Determine positions based on probabilities
for(i in seq_along(strain_names)) {
  # Position 1: Mitochondrial DNA
  if(sum(mprob[i,]) > 0) {
    cross_info[i,1] <- which.max(mprob[i,])
  }
  
  # Position 8: Y chromosome
  if(sum(yprob[i,]) > 0) {
    cross_info[i,8] <- which.max(yprob[i,])
  }
  
  # Handle case where positions 1 and 8 are the same
  if(!is.na(cross_info[i,1]) && !is.na(cross_info[i,8]) && 
     cross_info[i,1] == cross_info[i,8]) {
    # Get second highest Y probability
    y_vals <- yprob[i,]
    y_vals[cross_info[i,1]] <- -1
    cross_info[i,8] <- which.max(y_vals)
  }
  
  # Position 3: X chromosome (typically)
  if(sum(xprob[i,]) > 0) {
    # Get order of X probabilities
    x_order <- order(xprob[i,], decreasing=TRUE)
    
    # Remove founders already used
    used <- c()
    if(!is.na(cross_info[i,1])) used <- c(used, cross_info[i,1])
    if(!is.na(cross_info[i,8])) used <- c(used, cross_info[i,8])
    
    # First available high-probability X founder
    for(founder in x_order) {
      if(!(founder %in% used)) {
        cross_info[i,3] <- founder
        used <- c(used, founder)
        break
      }
    }
    
    # Positions 2, 5, 6: Other high X probabilities
    positions <- c(2, 5, 6)
    for(pos in positions) {
      for(founder in x_order) {
        if(!(founder %in% used)) {
          cross_info[i,pos] <- founder
          used <- c(used, founder)
          break
        }
      }
    }
    
    # Positions 4, 7: Remaining founders
    remain_pos <- c(4, 7)
    remain_founders <- setdiff(1:8, used)
    
    for(j in seq_along(remain_pos)) {
      if(j <= length(remain_founders)) {
        cross_info[i,remain_pos[j]] <- remain_founders[j]
      }
    }
  }
}

# Final check - make sure all positions have values 1-8
for(i in seq_along(strain_names)) {
  # Check for NA values and fill them
  na_pos <- which(is.na(cross_info[i,]))
  if(length(na_pos) > 0) {
    # Find which founders are already used
    used <- cross_info[i,-na_pos]
    used <- used[!is.na(used)]
    
    # Find available founders
    available <- setdiff(1:8, used)
    
    # Fill NA positions
    if(length(available) >= length(na_pos)) {
      cross_info[i,na_pos] <- available[1:length(na_pos)]
    } else {
      # In case of inconsistency, just assign sequential values
      cross_info[i,] <- 1:8
    }
  }
  
  # Check for duplicates
  if(length(unique(cross_info[i,])) < 8) {
    # If duplicates exist, just use 1:8
    cross_info[i,] <- 1:8
  }
}

# Map to your strain IDs
cross_info_new <- matrix(ncol=8, nrow=length(strain_mapping))
rownames(cross_info_new) <- strain_mapping
colnames(cross_info_new) <- LETTERS[1:8]

for(i in seq_along(strain_names)) {
  cross_info_new[strain_mapping[strain_names[i]],] <- cross_info[i,]
}

# Print the cross order
message("Cross order for each strain:")
for(i in seq_along(rownames(cross_info_new))) {
  strain <- rownames(cross_info_new)[i]
  order <- paste(cross_info_new[i,], collapse=", ")
  message(strain, ": ", order)
}

# Save cross_info with the proper strain IDs
cross_info_df <- as.data.frame(cross_info_new)
cross_info_df$id <- rownames(cross_info_df)
cross_info_df <- cross_info_df[, c("id", LETTERS[1:8])]
write.csv(cross_info_df, file.path(output_dir, "cc_crossinfo.csv"), row.names=FALSE)

# Create the control file
message("Creating control file")
chr <- c(1:19, "X")
write_control_file(file.path(output_dir, "cc_strains_final.json"), 
                  crosstype="risib8",
                  description="CC strains analysis with cross order from Prob36",
                  founder_geno_file=paste0("RawData/GM/GM_foundergeno", chr, ".csv"),
                  founder_geno_transposed=TRUE,
                  gmap_file=paste0("RawData/GM/GM_gmap", chr, ".csv"),
                  pmap_file=paste0("RawData/GM/GM_pmap", chr, ".csv"),
                  geno_file=paste0("cc_strains_geno", chr, ".csv"),
                  geno_transposed=TRUE,
                  geno_codes=list(A=1, H=2, B=3),
                  xchr="X",
                  crossinfo_file="cc_crossinfo.csv",
                  overwrite=TRUE)

message("Complete! Now you can calculate the founder probabilities.")

```

```{r}
# Load the final control file
cc_final <- read_cross2("Data/CCDiet/cc_strains_final.json")

# Calculate genotype probabilities
probs_final <- calc_genoprob(cc_final, error_prob=0.002)

# Save the probabilities
save(probs_final, file="Data/CCDiet/cc_founder_probs_final.RData")

# Done! This is the Founder_prob file I needed for comparison 
```

## DONE

## 5. Load in Metadata

```{r}
# Control Pups (70 samples)

# Load in Excel file and read the "ControlPups" sheet
ControlPup <- read.xlsx("/project/do2_projects/AFRI/GBRS_development/Data/Data4R_db_03102025_FINAL.xlsx", sheet = "ControlPups", na.strings = c(""," ","NA","N/A","na", "n/a"), skipEmptyRows = TRUE)

# Add Cross column 
ControlPup$Cross <- paste0(ControlPup$CC.Mom.Strain, "x", ControlPup$CC.Dad.Strain)

#Edit Low Protein to LP and AIN-93G to AIN (removing space/- since it messes up contrasts function)
ControlPup$Diet <- gsub("Low Protein", "LP", ControlPup$Diet)
ControlPup$Diet <- gsub("AIN-93G", "AIN", ControlPup$Diet)

metadata_subset = ControlPup
# Select only the required columns

```

```{r}
# CCRIX samples (176) 

# Load in Excel file and read the "pups" sheet
pup <- read.xlsx("Data/Data4R_db_03102025_FINAL.xlsx", sheet = "Pups", na.strings = c(""," ","NA","N/A","na", "n/a"), skipEmptyRows = TRUE)

# Add Cross column 
pup$Cross <- paste0(pup$CC.Mom.Strain, "x", pup$CC.Dad.Strain)

#Edit Low Protein to LP and AIN-93G to AIN (removing space/- since it messes up contrasts function)
pup$Diet <- gsub("Low Protein", "LP", pup$Diet)
pup$Diet <- gsub("AIN-93G", "AIN", pup$Diet)

metadata_subset = pup

```

## 6. Interpolate markers to gene positions

```{r}
## Interpolate CC Diplotype Probabilities to Gene Positions
## Modified script to use probs_final instead of filtered_CC_diplotype_probs

anno = read.csv("Data/gm_uwisc_v4.csv")

# Load required libraries
library(IRanges)
library(qtl2convert) # You might need to install this
library(qtl2)        # You might need to install this
library(data.table)
library(dplyr)

# Define interpolation functions
interpolate_one_chr = function(pr1, mkr1, mkr2) {
  
  # Verify that the marker names in pr1 and mkr1 are identical and in the same
  # order.
  stopifnot(dimnames(pr1)[[3]] == names(mkr1))
  
  # Verify that the markers are in bp and not Mbp.
  stopifnot(min(mkr1) > 200)
  stopifnot(min(mkr2) > 200)

  # Convert markers to IRanges to used inter-range functions.
  mkr1 = IRanges(start = mkr1, width = 1, names = names(mkr1))
  mkr2 = IRanges(start = mkr2, width = 1, names = names(mkr2))
  
  # The the proximal and distal markers in mkr1 which bracket the markers
  # in mkr2.
  proximal = follow(mkr2,  mkr1)
  distal   = precede(mkr2, mkr1)
  proximal[is.na(proximal)] = 1
  distal[is.na(distal)]     = length(mkr1)
  
  # Ensure indices are within the range
  proximal[proximal > dim(pr1)[3]] = dim(pr1)[3]
  distal[distal     > dim(pr1)[3]] = dim(pr1)[3]
  
  # Get the proximal and distal probs.
  prox_pr = pr1[,,proximal, drop = FALSE]
  dist_pr = pr1[,,distal,   drop = FALSE]
  
  # interpolate values
  mkr_interp = (start(mkr2) - start(mkr1)[proximal]) / (start(mkr1)[distal] - start(mkr1)[proximal])
  mkr_interp[is.infinite(mkr_interp)] = 1
  mkr_interp = array(rep(mkr_interp, each = nrow(pr1) * ncol(pr1)),
                     c(nrow(pr1), ncol(pr1), length(mkr2)),
                     dimnames = list(rownames(pr1), colnames(pr1), names(mkr2)))

  # Create the new probs array.
  tmp = prox_pr + (mkr_interp) * (dist_pr - prox_pr)
  dimnames(tmp)[[3]] = names(mkr2)
  return(tmp)
}

# Top-level interpolate function.
interpolate_genoprobs = function(probs1, markers1, markers2) {

  # Perform some minimal error checking. 
  if(length(probs1) != length(markers1)) {
    stop('Probs1 and markers1 must be the same length.')
  }

  # If the marker maps are in Mb, convert to bp.
  if(max(unlist(markers1) < 200)) {
    markers1 = lapply(markers1, '*', 1e6)
  }

  if(max(unlist(markers2) < 200)) {
    markers2 = lapply(markers2, '*', 1e6)
  }
  
  all_chr = names(probs1)

  new_probs1        = vector('list', length(probs1))
  names(new_probs1) = names(probs1)

  for(chr in all_chr) {
    new_probs1[[chr]] = interpolate_one_chr(pr1  = probs1[[chr]],
                                           mkr1 = markers1[[chr]],
                                           mkr2 = markers2[[chr]])
  }

  # Set the attributes for the probs object.
  attr(new_probs1, 'crosstype')    = attr(probs1, 'crosstype')
  attr(new_probs1, 'is_x_chr')     = attr(probs1, 'is_x_chr')
  attr(new_probs1, 'alleles')      = attr(probs1, 'alleles')
  attr(new_probs1, 'alleleprobs')  = attr(probs1, 'alleleprobs')
  class(new_probs1)                = class(probs1)
  
  return(new_probs1)
}

# Step 1: Print info about existing data objects
cat("Examining existing data objects...\n")
cat("Dimensions of annotation data:", dim(anno), "\n")

# Show chromosome distributions
cat("\nChromosome distribution in anno:\n")
print(table(anno$chr))

cat("\nChromosome distribution in genes_with_id:\n")
print(table(genes_with_id$chromosome_name))

cat("\nStructure of probs_final:\n")
for (chr in names(probs_final)) {
  chr_array <- probs_final[[chr]]
  cat("Chromosome", chr, "dimensions:", paste(dim(chr_array), collapse=" x "), "\n")
  cat("  Samples:", paste(dimnames(chr_array)[[1]], collapse=", "), "\n")
  cat("  Founders:", paste(dimnames(chr_array)[[2]], collapse=", "), "\n")
  cat("  First few markers:", paste(head(dimnames(chr_array)[[3]], 3), collapse=", "), "\n")
}

# Step 2: Format marker data into qtl2 format
cat("\nFormatting marker data...\n")
markers_list <- list()
skipped_chrs <- character(0)

for (chr in names(probs_final)) {
  # Get the chromosome number without "chr" prefix
  chr_num <- gsub("^chr", "", chr)
  
  # Find markers for this chromosome in anno
  anno_chr <- anno[anno$chr == chr_num, ]
  
  if (nrow(anno_chr) == 0) {
    cat("Warning: No markers found in anno for chromosome", chr, "\n")
    skipped_chrs <- c(skipped_chrs, chr)
    next
  }
  
  # Get marker positions
  marker_pos <- anno_chr$bp_mm10
  names(marker_pos) <- anno_chr$marker
  
  # Get marker names from probability data
  prob_markers <- dimnames(probs_final[[chr]])[[3]]
  
  # Check for marker name match
  matching_markers <- intersect(names(marker_pos), prob_markers)
  
  if (length(matching_markers) > 0) {
    cat("Found", length(matching_markers), "matching markers for chromosome", chr, "\n")
    
    # Keep only matching markers in the same order as in probability data
    matching_indices <- match(matching_markers, prob_markers)
    matching_markers_ordered <- prob_markers[sort(matching_indices)]
    
    # Create a subset of markers
    marker_pos_subset <- marker_pos[matching_markers_ordered]
    
    # Store the marker positions
    markers_list[[chr]] <- marker_pos_subset
  } else {
    cat("No matching marker names for chromosome", chr, "\n")
    
    # Instead of using synthetic positions, we'll try to map based on position ordering
    # This assumes that both lists are ordered by genomic position
    marker_count <- length(prob_markers)
    
    if (length(marker_pos) >= marker_count) {
      # Sample positions at regular intervals
      indices <- round(seq(1, length(marker_pos), length.out = marker_count))
      sampled_pos <- marker_pos[indices]
      names(sampled_pos) <- prob_markers
      markers_list[[chr]] <- sampled_pos
      cat("Mapped", length(sampled_pos), "markers using position-based assignment\n")
    } else {
      cat("Warning: Not enough markers in anno for chromosome", chr, "\n")
      cat("Skipping chromosome", chr, "due to insufficient marker data\n")
      skipped_chrs <- c(skipped_chrs, chr)
    }
  }
}

# Report on skipped chromosomes
if (length(skipped_chrs) > 0) {
  cat("\nThe following chromosomes were skipped due to insufficient marker data:\n")
  cat(paste(skipped_chrs, collapse=", "), "\n")
}

# Update to remove chromosomes with insufficient data
valid_chrs <- names(markers_list)
cat("\nChromosomes with valid marker data:", paste(valid_chrs, collapse=", "), "\n")

# Filter the probability data to only include chromosomes with sufficient marker data
# Don't use direct subsetting, which can break attributes
new_filtered_probs <- vector('list', length(valid_chrs))
names(new_filtered_probs) <- valid_chrs

for (chr in valid_chrs) {
  new_filtered_probs[[chr]] <- probs_final[[chr]]
}

# Copy over all attributes
attr(new_filtered_probs, 'crosstype') <- attr(probs_final, 'crosstype')
attr(new_filtered_probs, 'is_x_chr') <- attr(probs_final, 'is_x_chr')[valid_chrs]
attr(new_filtered_probs, 'alleles') <- attr(probs_final, 'alleles')
attr(new_filtered_probs, 'alleleprobs') <- attr(probs_final, 'alleleprobs')
class(new_filtered_probs) <- class(probs_final)

# Replace the original object
filtered_probs <- new_filtered_probs

# Step 3: Format gene positions into qtl2 format
cat("\nFormatting gene positions...\n")
genes_list <- list()
genes_skipped_chrs <- character(0)

for (chr in names(filtered_probs)) {
  # Get the chromosome number without "chr" prefix
  chr_num <- gsub("^chr", "", chr)
  
  # Find genes for this chromosome
  # Try different formats depending on how chromosome names are stored in genes_with_id
  gene_chr_formats <- c(
    chr_num,                # e.g., "1"
    chr,                    # e.g., "chr1"
    toupper(chr_num),       # e.g., "X"
    paste0("chr", toupper(chr_num))  # e.g., "chrX"
  )
  
  genes_found <- FALSE
  
  for (gene_chr in gene_chr_formats) {
    genes_chr <- genes_with_id[genes_with_id$chromosome_name == gene_chr, ]
    
    if (nrow(genes_chr) > 0) {
      cat("Found", nrow(genes_chr), "genes for chromosome", chr, "using format", gene_chr, "\n")
      
      # Use midpoint of gene as position
      gene_pos <- (genes_chr$start_position + genes_chr$end_position) / 2
      names(gene_pos) <- genes_chr$ensembl_gene_id
      
      # Store the genes for this chromosome
      genes_list[[chr]] <- gene_pos
      genes_found <- TRUE
      break
    }
  }
  
  if (!genes_found) {
    cat("Warning: No genes found for chromosome", chr, "\n")
    genes_skipped_chrs <- c(genes_skipped_chrs, chr)
  }
}

# Report on chromosomes with no gene data
if (length(genes_skipped_chrs) > 0) {
  cat("\nThe following chromosomes were skipped due to missing gene data:\n")
  cat(paste(genes_skipped_chrs, collapse=", "), "\n")
}

# Step 4: Check alignment between markers and genes
cat("\nChecking alignment between markers and genes...\n")
common_chrs <- intersect(names(markers_list), names(genes_list))
cat("Common chromosomes for interpolation:", paste(common_chrs, collapse=", "), "\n")

if (length(common_chrs) == 0) {
  stop("No common chromosomes found between markers and genes!")
}

# Filter to only common chromosomes
# Create new lists instead of subsetting the original objects
new_markers_list <- list()
new_genes_list <- list()
probs_list <- list()

for (chr in common_chrs) {
  new_markers_list[[chr]] <- markers_list[[chr]]
  new_genes_list[[chr]] <- genes_list[[chr]]
  probs_list[[chr]] <- filtered_probs[[chr]]
}

# Copy over attributes to probs_list
attr(probs_list, 'crosstype') <- attr(filtered_probs, 'crosstype')
attr(probs_list, 'is_x_chr') <- attr(filtered_probs, 'is_x_chr')[common_chrs]
attr(probs_list, 'alleles') <- attr(filtered_probs, 'alleles')
attr(probs_list, 'alleleprobs') <- attr(filtered_probs, 'alleleprobs')
class(probs_list) <- class(filtered_probs)

# Step 5: Ensure marker alignment for each chromosome
cat("\nChecking marker alignment for each chromosome...\n")
for (chr in common_chrs) {
  prob_markers <- dimnames(probs_list[[chr]])[[3]]
  marker_pos_markers <- names(new_markers_list[[chr]])
  
  if (!identical(prob_markers, marker_pos_markers)) {
    cat("Aligning markers for chromosome", chr, "\n")
    
    # Find the common markers
    common_markers <- intersect(prob_markers, marker_pos_markers)
    
    if (length(common_markers) > 0) {
      # Keep only common markers in both datasets
      prob_indices <- match(common_markers, prob_markers)
      probs_list[[chr]] <- probs_list[[chr]][,,prob_indices, drop=FALSE]
      
      # Keep the same markers in marker_pos
      new_markers_list[[chr]] <- new_markers_list[[chr]][common_markers]
      
      cat("  Aligned", length(common_markers), "markers for chromosome", chr, "\n")
    } else {
      # If no common markers but markers are the same length, we can try to use position ordering
      if (length(prob_markers) == length(marker_pos_markers)) {
        # If same length, just rename the marker positions
        names(new_markers_list[[chr]]) <- prob_markers
        cat("  Renamed markers in position list to match probability markers\n")
      } else {
        # If different lengths and no common markers, this is problematic
        cat("Warning: Cannot establish marker correspondence for chromosome", chr, "\n")
        cat("  Removing chromosome", chr, "from analysis\n")
        
        # Remove this chromosome from our lists
        new_markers_list[[chr]] <- NULL
        new_genes_list[[chr]] <- NULL
        probs_list[[chr]] <- NULL
      }
    }
  }
}

# Update common_chrs after alignment checks
common_chrs <- intersect(names(new_markers_list), names(new_genes_list))
cat("\nFinal chromosomes for interpolation after alignment checks:", paste(common_chrs, collapse=", "), "\n")

if (length(common_chrs) == 0) {
  stop("No common chromosomes left after alignment checks!")
}

# Double-check marker alignment for all remaining chromosomes
for (chr in common_chrs) {
  if (!identical(dimnames(probs_list[[chr]])[[3]], names(new_markers_list[[chr]]))) {
    stop("Failed to align markers for chromosome ", chr)
  }
}

# Step 6: Apply interpolation
cat("\nPerforming interpolation...\n")
interpolated_probs <- interpolate_genoprobs(
  probs1 = probs_list,
  markers1 = new_markers_list,
  markers2 = new_genes_list
)

# Step 7: Convert to a more convenient format for downstream analysis
cat("\nConverting interpolated probabilities to data frame...\n")
interpolated_df <- data.frame()

for (chr_name in names(interpolated_probs)) {
  chr_array <- interpolated_probs[[chr_name]]
  
  # Extract dimensions
  samples <- dimnames(chr_array)[[1]]
  founders <- dimnames(chr_array)[[2]]
  genes <- dimnames(chr_array)[[3]]
  
  cat("Processing chromosome", chr_name, "with", length(genes), "genes\n")
  
  # Create data frame more efficiently with pre-allocation
  chr_rows <- length(samples) * length(founders) * length(genes)
  chr_df <- data.frame(
    sample = character(chr_rows),
    founder = character(chr_rows),
    gene = character(chr_rows),
    chromosome = character(chr_rows),
    probability = numeric(chr_rows),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  row_idx <- 1
  for (sample_idx in 1:length(samples)) {
    sample <- samples[sample_idx]
    for (founder_idx in 1:length(founders)) {
      founder <- founders[founder_idx]
      
      # Process batches of genes for efficiency
      batch_size <- 1000
      for (start_idx in seq(1, length(genes), by = batch_size)) {
        end_idx <- min(start_idx + batch_size - 1, length(genes))
        batch_genes <- genes[start_idx:end_idx]
        batch_size_actual <- length(batch_genes)
        
        # Extract batch probabilities
        batch_probs <- chr_array[sample_idx, founder_idx, batch_genes]
        
        # Assign to pre-allocated dataframe
        batch_indices <- row_idx:(row_idx + batch_size_actual - 1)
        chr_df$sample[batch_indices] <- sample
        chr_df$founder[batch_indices] <- founder
        chr_df$gene[batch_indices] <- batch_genes
        chr_df$chromosome[batch_indices] <- sub("chr", "", chr_name)
        chr_df$probability[batch_indices] <- batch_probs
        
        row_idx <- row_idx + batch_size_actual
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Append to main dataframe
  interpolated_df <- rbind(interpolated_df, chr_df[1:(row_idx-1),])
}

# Step 8: Save the interpolated probabilities with timestamp
cat("\nSaving results...\n")

# Create directory if it doesn't exist
dir.create("Data/CCDiet/Interpolate", recursive = TRUE, showWarnings = FALSE)

save_rds_file <- "Data/CCDiet/Interpolate/CCDiet_gene_interpolated_probs.rds"

# Save both the array and dataframe formats
saveRDS(interpolated_probs, save_rds_file)
save_csv_file <- "Data/CCDiet/Interpolate/CCDiet_gene_interpolated_probs.csv"
data.table::fwrite(interpolated_df, save_csv_file)

cat("Saved interpolated probabilities to", save_rds_file, "\n")
cat("Saved interpolated probabilities as dataframe to", save_csv_file, "\n")

# Final report
cat("\nSummary of interpolation:\n")
cat("- Started with", length(names(probs_final)), "chromosomes in probability data\n")
cat("- Found marker data for", length(names(markers_list)), "chromosomes\n")
cat("- Found gene data for", length(names(genes_list)), "chromosomes\n")
cat("- Successfully interpolated", length(names(interpolated_probs)), "chromosomes\n")
```

## 7. Compare "CCDiet_gene_interpolated_probs" vs "CC_gene_interpolated_probs" – Cosine similarity

```{r}
################################################################################
# CC Strains Cosine Similarity Analysis
# Analyzes cosine similarity between CC and CCDiet interpolated probabilities
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# Fixed cosine similarity function with proper error handling
cos_sim = function(a, b) {
  v = rep(1L, ncol(a))
  numerator <- (a * b) %*% v
  denominator <- sqrt(a^2 %*% v * b^2 %*% v)
  
  # Handle division by zero
  result <- numerator / denominator
  
  # Replace NaN values with 0 (no similarity)
  result[is.nan(result)] <- 0
  
  return(mean(result, na.rm = TRUE))
}

# Enhanced cosine similarity that also returns similarity per gene
cos_sim_per_gene = function(a, b) {
  v = rep(1L, ncol(a))
  numerator <- (a * b) %*% v
  denominator <- sqrt(a^2 %*% v * b^2 %*% v)
  
  # Handle division by zero
  gene_sims <- numerator / denominator
  
  # Replace NaN values with 0 (no similarity)
  gene_sims[is.nan(gene_sims)] <- 0
  
  return(list(
    overall_sim = mean(gene_sims, na.rm = TRUE),
    per_gene_sim = gene_sims
  ))
}

# Updated extract_strain_probs function for interpolated_probs structure
extract_strain_probs <- function(interpolated_probs, strain_name, gene_ids = NULL) {
  # Create matrix for this strain - if gene_ids not provided, we'll extract all genes
  all_genes <- c()
  
  # First, collect all unique gene IDs if not provided
  if (is.null(gene_ids)) {
    for (chr in names(interpolated_probs)) {
      all_genes <- c(all_genes, dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]])
    }
    gene_ids <- unique(all_genes)
  }
  
  # Create matrix for this strain
  strain_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = length(gene_ids), ncol = 8)
  rownames(strain_matrix) <- gene_ids
  colnames(strain_matrix) <- LETTERS[1:8]
  
  # Find the strain's index in each chromosome's data
  for (chr in names(interpolated_probs)) {
    # Get strain index (row index) - assuming interpolated_probs structure
    # where first dimension is strain, with strain names in dimnames[[1]]
    strain_idx <- which(dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[1]] == strain_name)
    
    if (length(strain_idx) > 0) {
      # Get genes for this chromosome
      chr_genes <- intersect(gene_ids, dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]])
      
      # Extract probabilities for this strain and these genes
      for (gene in chr_genes) {
        if (gene %in% dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]]) {
          gene_idx <- match(gene, dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]])
          # Extract the probability array for this strain and gene
          strain_matrix[gene, ] <- interpolated_probs[[chr]][strain_idx[1], , gene_idx]
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(strain_matrix)
}

# Function to analyze cosine similarity between two strain matrices
analyze_strain_cosine <- function(strain_matrix1, strain_matrix2, strain1_name, strain2_name) {
  # Ensure matrices have the same rows (genes)
  common_genes <- intersect(rownames(strain_matrix1), rownames(strain_matrix2))
  
  if (length(common_genes) == 0) {
    cat("No common genes found for comparison between", strain1_name, "and", strain2_name, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Subset matrices to common genes
  strain_matrix1 <- strain_matrix1[common_genes, ]
  strain_matrix2 <- strain_matrix2[common_genes, ]
  
  # Remove rows that are all zeros in any matrix
  non_zero_rows <- rowSums(strain_matrix1) > 0 & rowSums(strain_matrix2) > 0
  
  if (sum(non_zero_rows) == 0) {
    cat("ERROR: No valid non-zero rows found for comparison\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  strain_matrix1 <- strain_matrix1[non_zero_rows, ]
  strain_matrix2 <- strain_matrix2[non_zero_rows, ]
  
  # Calculate similarity
  sim <- cos_sim(strain_matrix1, strain_matrix2)
  
  # Calculate per-gene similarities for detailed analysis
  detailed_sim <- cos_sim_per_gene(strain_matrix1, strain_matrix2)
  
  # Calculate similarity thresholds for the cosine similarity
  gene_similarities <- data.frame(
    gene = rownames(strain_matrix1),
    similarity = detailed_sim$per_gene_sim,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Map genes to chromosomes
  chromosomes <- rep("unknown", length(gene_similarities$gene))
  names(chromosomes) <- gene_similarities$gene
  
  # For each chromosome in interpolated_probs1 (we'll use CC_gene_interpolated_probs)
  for (chr in names(CC_gene_interpolated_probs)) {
    # Get genes in this chromosome from third dimension of array
    chr_genes <- dimnames(CC_gene_interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]]
    
    # Find matching genes between our gene set and this chromosome
    matching_genes <- intersect(gene_similarities$gene, chr_genes)
    
    # Set chromosome for these genes
    if (length(matching_genes) > 0) {
      chromosomes[matching_genes] <- paste0("chr", chr)
    }
  }
  
  # Add chromosome information to the gene_similarities dataframe
  gene_similarities$chromosome <- chromosomes[gene_similarities$gene]
  
  # Calculate standard deviation of gene-level similarities
  gene_similarity_sd <- sd(as.vector(gene_similarities$similarity), na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Calculate per-chromosome similarity and standard deviation
  # Convert to proper data frame structure first to avoid matrix column issues
  gene_similarities_df <- data.frame(
    gene = as.character(gene_similarities$gene),
    similarity = as.vector(gene_similarities$similarity),
    chromosome = as.character(gene_similarities$chromosome),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  chr_similarities <- gene_similarities_df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(chromosome) %>%
    dplyr::summarize(
      similarity = mean(similarity, na.rm = TRUE),
      similarity_sd = sd(similarity, na.rm = TRUE),
      gene_count = dplyr::n(),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  # Create final result object
  result <- list(
    strain1_name = strain1_name,
    strain2_name = strain2_name,
    n_genes = nrow(strain_matrix1),
    similarity = sim,
    similarity_sd = gene_similarity_sd,
    
    # Detailed results for further analysis
    gene_similarities = gene_similarities,
    chr_similarities = chr_similarities
  )
  
  return(result)
}

# Function to compare multiple strain pairs
compare_strain_pairs <- function(cc_pairs, cc_interpolated_probs, diet_interpolated_probs, 
                                output_base_dir = "Results/CCComparison") {
  results <- list()
  
  # Ensure output directory exists
  if (!dir.exists(output_base_dir)) {
    dir.create(output_base_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  }
  
  # Process each strain pair
  for (i in 1:length(cc_pairs)) {
    cc_strain <- names(cc_pairs)[i]
    diet_strain <- cc_pairs[[i]]
    
    cat("\nProcessing comparison between", cc_strain, "and", diet_strain, "\n")
    
    # Extract strain matrices
    cc_matrix <- extract_strain_probs(cc_interpolated_probs, cc_strain)
    diet_matrix <- extract_strain_probs(diet_interpolated_probs, diet_strain)
    
    # Analyze cosine similarity
    comparison_result <- analyze_strain_cosine(cc_matrix, diet_matrix, cc_strain, diet_strain)
    
    if (!is.null(comparison_result)) {
      # Store results
      results[[paste(cc_strain, "vs", diet_strain)]] <- comparison_result
      
      cat("Analyzed", comparison_result$n_genes, "genes\n")
      cat("Cosine similarity:", round(comparison_result$similarity, 3), 
          "±", round(comparison_result$similarity_sd, 3), "\n")
      
      # Generate visualizations
      
      # 1. Chromosome-level similarity plot with error bars
      if (!is.null(comparison_result$chr_similarities) && 
          nrow(comparison_result$chr_similarities) > 0) {
        
        # Sort chromosomes to ensure proper ordering (chr1, chr2, ..., chr19, chrX)
        comparison_result$chr_similarities$chromosome <- factor(
          comparison_result$chr_similarities$chromosome, 
          levels = c(paste0("chr", 1:19), "chrX")
        )
        
        chr_summary <- comparison_result$chr_similarities %>% arrange(chromosome)
        
        # Create chromosome plot with error bars
        chr_plot <- ggplot(chr_summary, aes(x = chromosome, y = similarity)) +
          geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.7) +
          geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = similarity - similarity_sd, 
                           ymax = similarity + similarity_sd),
                       width = 0.3, alpha = 0.6) +
          theme_minimal() +
          labs(
            title = paste("Cosine Similarity by Chromosome -", 
                         cc_strain, "vs", diet_strain),
            y = "Similarity (± SD)",
            x = "Chromosome"
          ) +
          geom_hline(yintercept = comparison_result$similarity, 
                    color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
          theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
        
        # Save the chromosome plot
        plot_filename <- file.path(output_base_dir, 
                                paste0(cc_strain, "_vs_", diet_strain, "_chromosome_similarities.pdf"))
        ggsave(plot_filename, chr_plot, width = 10, height = 6)
        
        cat("Chromosome similarity plot saved to:", plot_filename, "\n")
      }
      
      # 2. Generate summary report
      report_filename <- file.path(output_base_dir, 
                                  paste0(cc_strain, "_vs_", diet_strain, "_report.txt"))
      
      sink(report_filename)
      cat("CC Strain Comparison Cosine Similarity Analysis\n")
      cat(paste(rep("=", 50), collapse = ""), "\n\n")
      cat("Analysis Date:", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "\n\n")
      
      cat("CC Strain 1:", cc_strain, "\n")
      cat("CC Strain 2:", diet_strain, "\n")
      cat("Common Genes Analyzed:", comparison_result$n_genes, "\n\n")
      
      cat("COSINE SIMILARITY METRICS:\n")
      cat("Overall similarity:", round(comparison_result$similarity, 3), "\n")
      cat("Overall SD:", round(comparison_result$similarity_sd, 3), "\n\n")
      
      # Add chromosome-level results to the report
      if (!is.null(comparison_result$chr_similarities) && 
          nrow(comparison_result$chr_similarities) > 0) {
        cat("\nCHROMOSOME-LEVEL RESULTS:\n")
        cat("Chromosome | Similarity | SD | Gene Count\n")
        cat(paste(rep("-", 50), collapse = ""), "\n")
        for (i in 1:nrow(chr_summary)) {
          cat(sprintf("%-10s | %10.3f | %6.3f | %10d\n",
                     chr_summary$chromosome[i],
                     chr_summary$similarity[i],
                     chr_summary$similarity_sd[i],
                     chr_summary$gene_count[i]))
        }
      }
      
      sink()
      
      cat("Report saved to:", report_filename, "\n")
      
      # Save raw results
      rds_filename <- file.path(output_base_dir, 
                               paste0(cc_strain, "_vs_", diet_strain, "_results.rds"))
      saveRDS(comparison_result, rds_filename)
    }
  }
  
  # Create overall summary
  if (length(results) > 0) {
    # Create comparison table
    comparison_table <- data.frame(
      Strain_Pair = character(),
      Similarity = numeric(),
      SD = numeric(),
      Genes_Analyzed = integer(),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    for (pair_name in names(results)) {
      result <- results[[pair_name]]
      
      comparison_table <- rbind(comparison_table, data.frame(
        Strain_Pair = pair_name,
        Similarity = round(result$similarity, 3),
        SD = round(result$similarity_sd, 3),
        Genes_Analyzed = result$n_genes,
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      ))
    }
    
    # Save overall summary
    summary_filename <- file.path(output_base_dir, "overall_comparison_summary.txt")
    
    sink(summary_filename)
    cat("CC Strain Comparison Overall Summary\n")
    cat("==================================\n\n")
    cat("Analysis Date:", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "\n\n")
    
    cat("STRAIN PAIR COMPARISONS:\n")
    print(comparison_table)
    
    # Generate overall visualization if multiple comparisons
    if (nrow(comparison_table) > 1) {
      # Create a bar plot for similarity across pairs with error bars
      comparison_table$Strain_Pair <- factor(comparison_table$Strain_Pair,
                                          levels = comparison_table$Strain_Pair[order(comparison_table$Similarity, decreasing = TRUE)])
      
      overall_plot <- ggplot(comparison_table, aes(x = Strain_Pair, y = Similarity)) +
        geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "blue", alpha = 0.7) +
        geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Similarity - SD, ymax = Similarity + SD),
                     width = 0.3, alpha = 0.6) +
        theme_minimal() +
        labs(
          title = "Cosine Similarity Comparison Across Strain Pairs",
          y = "Similarity (± SD)",
          x = "Strain Pairs"
        ) +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
      
      # Save the overall comparison plot
      overall_plot_filename <- file.path(output_base_dir, "overall_similarity_comparison.pdf")
      ggsave(overall_plot_filename, overall_plot, width = 10, height = 6)
      
      cat("\nOverall comparison plot saved to:", overall_plot_filename, "\n")
    }
    
    sink()
    
    cat("Overall summary saved to:", summary_filename, "\n")
    
    # Save all results
    saveRDS(results, file.path(output_base_dir, "all_comparison_results.rds"))
  }
  
  return(results)
}

#################################################################################
# MAIN ANALYSIS SCRIPT
#################################################################################

# Define strain pairs for comparison
cc_pairs <- list(
  "CC001/Unc" = "CC-276",
  "CC019/TauUnc" = "CC-309",
  "CC040/TauUnc" = "CC-284"
)

# Define output directory
output_dir <- "Data/CCDiet/CCDiet_Comparison/UNC"

# Run the comparison analysis
results <- compare_strain_pairs(
  cc_pairs = cc_pairs,
  cc_interpolated_probs = CC_gene_interpolated_probs,
  diet_interpolated_probs = CCDiet_gene_interpolated_probs,
  output_base_dir = output_dir
)

# Print final message
cat("\nAnalysis complete. Results and visualizations saved to:", output_dir, "\n")

## Very concordant!!
# CC001: 0.986 +- 0.11
# CC019: 0.954 +- 0.202
# CC040: 0.965 +- 0.168
```

## 8. Compare GBRS-derived CC diplotypes vs "CCDiet_gene_interpolated_probs" – Cosine similarity

```{r}
################################################################################
# CC Parental Baseline Cosine Similarity Analysis
# Analyzes GBRS CC parental samples for baseline metrics against CCDiet interpolated probs
# Updated to include standard deviation calculations
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# Fixed cosine similarity function with proper error handling
cos_sim = function(a, b) {
  v = rep(1L, ncol(a))
  numerator <- (a * b) %*% v
  denominator <- sqrt(a^2 %*% v * b^2 %*% v)
  
  # Handle division by zero
  result <- numerator / denominator
  
  # Replace NaN values with 0 (no similarity)
  result[is.nan(result)] <- 0
  
  return(mean(result, na.rm = TRUE))
}

# Enhanced cosine similarity that also returns similarity per gene
cos_sim_per_gene = function(a, b) {
  v = rep(1L, ncol(a))
  numerator <- (a * b) %*% v
  denominator <- sqrt(a^2 %*% v * b^2 %*% v)
  
  # Handle division by zero
  gene_sims <- numerator / denominator
  
  # Replace NaN values with 0 (no similarity)
  gene_sims[is.nan(gene_sims)] <- 0
  
  return(list(
    overall_sim = mean(gene_sims, na.rm = TRUE),
    per_gene_sim = gene_sims
  ))
}

# Convert GBRS read counts to allele proportions matrix
gbrs_to_proportions <- function(gbrs_counts) {
  # Initialize the proportions matrix
  prop_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(gbrs_counts), ncol = 8)
  rownames(prop_matrix) <- gbrs_counts$locus
  colnames(prop_matrix) <- LETTERS[1:8]
  
  # Fill in proportions
  for (i in 1:nrow(gbrs_counts)) {
    if (!is.na(gbrs_counts$total[i]) && gbrs_counts$total[i] > 0) {
      prop_matrix[i, ] <- as.numeric(gbrs_counts[i, LETTERS[1:8]]) / gbrs_counts$total[i]
    }
  }
  
  return(prop_matrix)
}

# Extract strain probabilities from interpolated probabilities matrix
extract_strain_probs <- function(interpolated_probs, strain_name, gene_ids = NULL) {
  # Create matrix for this strain - if gene_ids not provided, we'll extract all genes
  all_genes <- c()
  
  # First, collect all unique gene IDs if not provided
  if (is.null(gene_ids)) {
    for (chr in names(interpolated_probs)) {
      all_genes <- c(all_genes, dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]])
    }
    gene_ids <- unique(all_genes)
  }
  
  # Create matrix for this strain
  strain_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = length(gene_ids), ncol = 8)
  rownames(strain_matrix) <- gene_ids
  colnames(strain_matrix) <- LETTERS[1:8]
  
  # Find the strain's index in each chromosome's data
  for (chr in names(interpolated_probs)) {
    # Get strain index (row index) - assuming interpolated_probs structure
    # where first dimension is strain, with strain names in dimnames[[1]]
    strain_idx <- which(dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[1]] == strain_name)
    
    if (length(strain_idx) > 0) {
      # Get genes for this chromosome
      chr_genes <- intersect(gene_ids, dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]])
      
      # Extract probabilities for this strain and these genes
      for (gene in chr_genes) {
        if (gene %in% dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]]) {
          gene_idx <- match(gene, dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]])
          # Extract the probability array for this strain and gene
          strain_matrix[gene, ] <- interpolated_probs[[chr]][strain_idx[1], , gene_idx]
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(strain_matrix)
}

# Analyze cosine similarity for a single CC parental sample against CCDiet
analyze_sample_cosine <- function(sample_id, gbrs_base_path, diet_interpolated_probs, diet_strain_id) {
  # Construct file path for CC parental sample
  sample_file <- file.path(gbrs_base_path, sample_id, 
                          paste0(sample_id, "_gbrs.quantified.diploid.genes.expected_read_counts"))
  
  # Check if file exists
  if (!file.exists(sample_file)) {
    cat("File not found for sample", sample_id, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Load GBRS alignment counts
```
