```{r}
  gbrs_counts <- tryCatch({
    read.table(sample_file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error reading file for sample", sample_id, ":", e$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  if (is.null(gbrs_counts) || nrow(gbrs_counts) == 0) {
    cat("Empty file for sample", sample_id, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Check required columns
  required_cols <- c("locus", "total", LETTERS[1:8])
  if (!all(required_cols %in% colnames(gbrs_counts))) {
    cat("Missing required columns for sample", sample_id, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Convert GBRS data to proportions matrix
  sample_matrix <- gbrs_to_proportions(gbrs_counts)
  
  # Get gene IDs to analyze (those in the GBRS file with non-zero totals)
  valid_genes <- gbrs_counts$locus[!is.na(gbrs_counts$total) & gbrs_counts$total > 0]
  
  # Extract strain matrix for the diet strain
  diet_matrix <- extract_strain_probs(diet_interpolated_probs, diet_strain_id, valid_genes)
  
  # Ensure matrices have the same rows (genes)
  common_genes <- intersect(rownames(sample_matrix), rownames(diet_matrix))
  
  if (length(common_genes) == 0) {
    cat("No common genes found for sample", sample_id, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Subset matrices to common genes
  sample_matrix <- sample_matrix[common_genes, ]
  diet_matrix <- diet_matrix[common_genes, ]
  
  # Remove rows that are all zeros in any matrix
  non_zero_rows <- rowSums(sample_matrix) > 0 & rowSums(diet_matrix) > 0
  
  if (sum(non_zero_rows) == 0) {
    cat("ERROR: No valid non-zero rows found for comparison\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  sample_matrix <- sample_matrix[non_zero_rows, ]
  diet_matrix <- diet_matrix[non_zero_rows, ]
  
  # Calculate similarity
  sim_to_diet <- cos_sim(sample_matrix, diet_matrix)
  
  # Calculate per-gene similarities for detailed analysis
  detailed_sim_diet <- cos_sim_per_gene(sample_matrix, diet_matrix)
  
  # Calculate similarity thresholds for the cosine similarity
  gene_similarities <- data.frame(
    gene = rownames(sample_matrix),
    sim_diet = detailed_sim_diet$per_gene_sim,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Map genes to chromosomes using the diet_interpolated_probs structure
  chromosomes <- rep("unknown", length(gene_similarities$gene))
  names(chromosomes) <- gene_similarities$gene
  
  # For each chromosome in the diet_interpolated_probs list
  for (chr in names(diet_interpolated_probs)) {
    # Get genes in this chromosome from third dimension of array
    chr_genes <- dimnames(diet_interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]]
    
    # Find matching genes between our gene set and this chromosome
    matching_genes <- intersect(gene_similarities$gene, chr_genes)
    
    # Set chromosome for these genes
    if (length(matching_genes) > 0) {
      chromosomes[matching_genes] <- paste0("chr", chr)
    }
  }
  
  # Add chromosome information to the gene_similarities dataframe
  gene_similarities$chromosome <- chromosomes[gene_similarities$gene]
  
  # Calculate standard deviation of gene-level similarities
  gene_similarity_sd <- sd(as.vector(gene_similarities$sim_diet), na.rm = TRUE)
  
  # Calculate per-chromosome similarity and standard deviation
  # Convert to proper data frame structure first to avoid matrix column issues
  gene_similarities_df <- data.frame(
    gene = as.character(gene_similarities$gene),
    sim_diet = as.vector(gene_similarities$sim_diet),
    chromosome = as.character(gene_similarities$chromosome),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  chr_similarities <- gene_similarities_df %>%
    dplyr::group_by(chromosome) %>%
    dplyr::summarize(
      sim_diet = mean(sim_diet, na.rm = TRUE),
      sim_diet_sd = sd(sim_diet, na.rm = TRUE),
      gene_count = dplyr::n(),
      .groups = 'drop'
    )
  
  # Create final result object
  result <- list(
    sample_id = sample_id,
    diet_strain_id = diet_strain_id,
    n_genes = nrow(sample_matrix),
    sim_to_diet = sim_to_diet,
    sim_to_diet_sd = gene_similarity_sd,
    
    # Detailed results for further analysis
    gene_similarities = gene_similarities,
    chr_similarities = chr_similarities
  )
  
  return(result)
}

# Function to analyze all parental samples
analyze_all_parental_samples <- function(sample_list, gbrs_base_path, metadata_subset, 
                                     diet_interpolated_probs, diet_strains) {
  results <- list()
  
  # Initialize results list for each CC line
  for (strain in unique(names(diet_strains))) {
    results[[strain]] <- list()
  }
  
  for (sample_id in sample_list) {
    # Find this sample in the metadata using exact matching of ID as character
    sample_row <- which(as.character(metadata_subset$Animal.ID) == sample_id)
    
    if (length(sample_row) == 0) {
      cat("Skipping sample", sample_id, "- not found in metadata\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Get the cross type from metadata
    cross_type <- metadata_subset$Cross[sample_row[1]]
    
    # Skip if cross type not in diet_strains
    if (!(cross_type %in% names(diet_strains))) {
      cat("Skipping sample", sample_id, "- cross type", cross_type, "not defined in diet_strains\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Get diet strain ID for this cross
    diet_strain_id <- diet_strains[[cross_type]]
    
    cat("Processing sample", sample_id, "(Cross:", cross_type, ", Diet Strain:", diet_strain_id, ")\n")
    
    # Analyze this sample against its diet strain
    sample_result <- analyze_sample_cosine(sample_id, gbrs_base_path, 
                                          diet_interpolated_probs, diet_strain_id)
    
    if (!is.null(sample_result)) {
      results[[cross_type]][[sample_id]] <- sample_result
      cat("Analyzed", sample_result$n_genes, "genes for sample", sample_id, "\n")
      cat("Cosine similarity to", diet_strain_id, ":", 
          round(sample_result$sim_to_diet, 3), "±", 
          round(sample_result$sim_to_diet_sd, 3), "\n")
    }
  }
  
  return(results)
}

# Summarize cosine similarity results for parental samples
summarize_parental_results <- function(cosine_results, diet_strains) {
  summary <- list()
  
  for (strain_cross in names(cosine_results)) {
    # Skip if no samples for this strain
    if (length(cosine_results[[strain_cross]]) == 0) {
      next
    }
    
    cat("\n=======================================\n")
    cat("Parental Strain:", strain_cross, "\n")
    
    # Get diet strain ID for this cross
    diet_strain_id <- diet_strains[[strain_cross]]
    
    cat("Diet Strain ID:", diet_strain_id, "\n")
    
    # Collect statistics
    sample_count <- length(cosine_results[[strain_cross]])
    cat("Number of samples:", sample_count, "\n")
    
    # Aggregate results
    sim_to_diet <- sapply(cosine_results[[strain_cross]], function(x) x$sim_to_diet)
    sim_to_diet_sd <- sapply(cosine_results[[strain_cross]], function(x) x$sim_to_diet_sd)
    genes_analyzed <- sapply(cosine_results[[strain_cross]], function(x) x$n_genes)
    
    cat("Total genes analyzed:", sum(genes_analyzed), "\n")
    cat("Average genes per sample:", round(mean(genes_analyzed)), "\n\n")
    
    cat("=== COSINE SIMILARITY METRICS ===\n")
    cat("Average similarity to", diet_strain_id, ":", round(mean(sim_to_diet), 3), "\n")
    cat("Min similarity:", round(min(sim_to_diet), 3), "\n")
    cat("Max similarity:", round(max(sim_to_diet), 3), "\n")
    cat("Standard deviation (across samples):", round(sd(sim_to_diet), 3), "\n")
    cat("Average within-sample SD:", round(mean(sim_to_diet_sd), 3), "\n\n")
    
    # Create data frame for sample-level results
    sample_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(cosine_results[[strain_cross]]), function(sample) {
      result <- cosine_results[[strain_cross]][[sample]]
      df <- data.frame(
        Sample = sample,
        Genes = result$n_genes,
        Sim_To_Diet = round(result$sim_to_diet, 3),
        Within_Sample_SD = round(result$sim_to_diet_sd, 3),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      return(df)
    }))
    
    # Aggregate chromosome-level results
    chr_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(cosine_results[[strain_cross]]), function(sample) {
      result <- cosine_results[[strain_cross]][[sample]]
      if (!is.null(result$chr_similarities)) {
        df <- result$chr_similarities
        df$Sample <- sample
        return(df)
      } else {
        return(NULL)
      }
    }))
    
    # Store summary results
    summary[[strain_cross]] <- list(
      strain_cross = strain_cross,
      diet_strain_id = diet_strain_id,
      sample_count = sample_count,
      total_genes = sum(genes_analyzed),
      avg_genes_per_sample = mean(genes_analyzed),
      
      # Cosine similarity metrics
      avg_sim_to_diet = mean(sim_to_diet),
      min_sim_to_diet = min(sim_to_diet),
      max_sim_to_diet = max(sim_to_diet),
      sd_sim_to_diet = sd(sim_to_diet),
      avg_within_sample_sd = mean(sim_to_diet_sd),
      
      # Sample-level results
      sample_results = sample_df,
      
      # Chromosome-level results
      chr_results = chr_df
    )
    
    # Print sample results
    cat("\n=== SAMPLE-LEVEL RESULTS ===\n")
    print(sample_df)
    
    # Print chromosome results summary
    cat("\n=== CHROMOSOME-LEVEL RESULTS ===\n")
    chr_summary <- chr_df %>%
      dplyr::group_by(chromosome) %>%
      dplyr::summarize(
        avg_sim = mean(sim_diet, na.rm = TRUE),
        sd_sim_across_samples = sd(sim_diet, na.rm = TRUE),
        avg_within_sample_sd = mean(sim_diet_sd, na.rm = TRUE),
        avg_genes = mean(gene_count, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = 'drop'
      )
    print(chr_summary)
  }
  
  return(summary)
}

# Modified visualization function to include chromosome-level visualization with error bars
create_parental_visualizations <- function(summary, output_base_dir = "Data/CCDiet/CCDiet_Comparison/CC") {
  viz_dirs <- list()
  
  for (strain_cross in names(summary)) {
    strain_data <- summary[[strain_cross]]
    diet_strain_id <- strain_data$diet_strain_id
    
    # Extract the CC number from the strain string for cleaner labels
    diet_strain_label <- diet_strain_id
    
    # Create strain-specific directory structure: Results/CCDiet/[strain]/Cosine
    strain_dir <- file.path(output_base_dir, strain_cross, "Cosine")
    if (!dir.exists(strain_dir)) {
      dir.create(strain_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    }
    
    # Store the visualization directory for this strain
    viz_dirs[[strain_cross]] <- strain_dir
    
    # Create visualizations only if we have sample results
    if (nrow(strain_data$sample_results) > 0) {
      # 1. Sample-by-sample bar plot with error bars
      sample_data <- strain_data$sample_results %>%
        dplyr::mutate(
          Sample = factor(Sample, levels = Sample[order(Sim_To_Diet, decreasing = TRUE)])
        )
      
      sample_plot <- ggplot(sample_data, aes(x = Sample, y = Sim_To_Diet)) +
        geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "blue", alpha = 0.7) +
        geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Sim_To_Diet - Within_Sample_SD, 
                         ymax = Sim_To_Diet + Within_Sample_SD),
                     width = 0.3, alpha = 0.6) +
        theme_minimal() +
        labs(
          title = paste("Cosine Similarity by Sample -", strain_cross),
          y = paste("Similarity to", diet_strain_label, "(± SD)"),
          x = "Sample ID"
        ) +
        geom_hline(yintercept = strain_data$avg_sim_to_diet, 
                  color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5))
      
      # Save the sample plot
      sample_plot_file <- file.path(strain_dir, paste0(strain_cross, "_sample_similarities.pdf"))
      ggsave(sample_plot_file, sample_plot, width = 12, height = 6)
      
      # 2. Chromosome-level similarity plot with error bars
      if (!is.null(strain_data$chr_results) && nrow(strain_data$chr_results) > 0) {
        # Aggregate by chromosome
        chr_summary <- strain_data$chr_results %>%
          dplyr::group_by(chromosome) %>%
          dplyr::summarize(
            avg_sim = mean(sim_diet, na.rm = TRUE),
            sd_sim_across_samples = sd(sim_diet, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_within_sample_sd = mean(sim_diet_sd, na.rm = TRUE),
            count = dplyr::n(),
            .groups = 'drop'
          )
        
        # Sort chromosomes to ensure proper ordering (chr1, chr2, ..., chr19, chrX)
        chr_summary$chromosome <- factor(chr_summary$chromosome, 
                                        levels = c(paste0("chr", 1:19), "chrX"))
        
        chr_summary <- chr_summary %>% arrange(chromosome)
        
        # Create chromosome plot with error bars showing within-sample SD
        chr_plot <- ggplot(chr_summary, aes(x = chromosome, y = avg_sim)) +
          geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.7) +
          geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = avg_sim - avg_within_sample_sd, 
                           ymax = avg_sim + avg_within_sample_sd), 
                       width = 0.3, alpha = 0.6) +
          theme_minimal() +
          labs(
            title = paste("Cosine Similarity by Chromosome -", strain_cross),
            y = paste("Similarity to", diet_strain_label, "(± avg within-sample SD)"),
            x = "Chromosome"
          ) +
          geom_hline(yintercept = strain_data$avg_sim_to_diet, 
                    color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
          theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
        
        # Save the chromosome plot
        chr_plot_file <- file.path(strain_dir, paste0(strain_cross, "_chromosome_similarities.pdf"))
        ggsave(chr_plot_file, chr_plot, width = 10, height = 6)
      }
    }
    
    cat("Visualizations for strain", strain_cross, "created in directory:", strain_dir, "\n")
  }
  
  # Create a comparison plot if we have multiple strains
  if (length(summary) > 1) {
    # Prepare comparison data for chromosome-level comparison across strains
    chr_comparison_data <- data.frame(
      Strain = character(),
      Chromosome = character(),
      Similarity = numeric(),
      SD = numeric(),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    )
    
    for (strain_cross in names(summary)) {
      strain_data <- summary[[strain_cross]]
      if (!is.null(strain_data$chr_results) && nrow(strain_data$chr_results) > 0) {
        # Aggregate chromosome data for this strain
        chr_summary <- strain_data$chr_results %>%
          dplyr::group_by(chromosome) %>%
          dplyr::summarize(
            avg_sim = mean(sim_diet, na.rm = TRUE),
            avg_within_sample_sd = mean(sim_diet_sd, na.rm = TRUE),
            .groups = 'drop'
          )
        
        temp_df <- data.frame(
          Strain = rep(strain_cross, nrow(chr_summary)),
          Chromosome = chr_summary$chromosome,
          Similarity = chr_summary$avg_sim,
          SD = chr_summary$avg_within_sample_sd,
          stringsAsFactors = FALSE
        )
        
        chr_comparison_data <- rbind(chr_comparison_data, temp_df)
      }
    }
    
    # Create chromosome comparison plot if we have data
    if (nrow(chr_comparison_data) > 0) {
      # Ensure proper chromosome ordering
      chr_comparison_data$Chromosome <- factor(chr_comparison_data$Chromosome, 
                                            levels = c(paste0("chr", 1:19), "chrX"))
      
      # Create the comparison plot
      chr_compare_plot <- ggplot(chr_comparison_data, 
                              aes(x = Chromosome, y = Similarity, fill = Strain)) +
        geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge", alpha = 0.7) +
        geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = Similarity - SD, ymax = Similarity + SD), 
                     position = position_dodge(width = 0.9), width = 0.2, alpha = 0.6) +
        theme_minimal() +
        labs(
          title = "Chromosome Similarity Comparison Across Strains",
          y = "Cosine Similarity (± avg within-sample SD)",
          x = "Chromosome"
        ) +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
      
      # Save the chromosome comparison plot
      chr_compare_file <- file.path(output_base_dir, "chromosome_strain_comparison.pdf")
      ggsave(chr_compare_file, chr_compare_plot, width = 12, height = 6)
      
      cat("Chromosome comparison plot created at:", chr_compare_file, "\n")
    }
  }
  
  return(viz_dirs)
}

#################################################################################
# MAIN ANALYSIS SCRIPT
#################################################################################

# Define strain pairs for comparison (CC cross types to CCDiet strains)
diet_strains <- list(
  "CC001xCC001" = "CC-276", 
  "CC019xCC019" = "CC-309",
  "CC040xCC040" = "CC-284"
)

# Define sample list from metadata - ensure they're character strings
sample_list <- as.character(metadata_subset$Animal.ID)

# Define base path to GBRS data
gbrs_base_path <- "CC_quantify_ASE"

# Define output directory base
output_base_dir <- "Data/CCDiet/CCDiet_Comparison/CC"

# Run the analysis
results <- analyze_all_parental_samples(
  sample_list = sample_list,
  gbrs_base_path = gbrs_base_path,
  metadata_subset = metadata_subset,
  diet_interpolated_probs = CCDiet_gene_interpolated_probs,
  diet_strains = diet_strains
)

# Summarize results
summary <- summarize_parental_results(results, diet_strains)

# Create visualizations - saves to Results/CCDiet/[strain]/Cosine
viz_dirs <- create_parental_visualizations(summary, output_base_dir)

# Generate summary reports for each strain
for (strain_cross in names(summary)) {
  # Create strain-specific directory for output
  strain_cosine_dir <- file.path(output_base_dir, strain_cross, "Cosine")
  if (!dir.exists(strain_cosine_dir)) {
    dir.create(strain_cosine_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  }
  
  # Generate report for this strain
  strain_data <- summary[[strain_cross]]
  report_file <- file.path(strain_cosine_dir, paste0(strain_cross, "_cosine_analysis_report.txt"))
  
  sink(report_file)
  cat("CC Parental Baseline Cosine Similarity Analysis for", strain_cross, "\n")
  cat(paste(rep("=", 50), collapse = ""), "\n\n")
  cat("Analysis Date:", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "\n")
  
  # Write strain-specific information
  cat("\nCC Strain Cross:", strain_cross, "\n")
  cat("Diet Strain ID:", strain_data$diet_strain_id, "\n")
  cat("Number of Samples:", strain_data$sample_count, "\n")
  cat("Total Genes Analyzed:", strain_data$total_genes, "\n")
  cat("Average Genes per Sample:", round(strain_data$avg_genes_per_sample), "\n\n")
  
  cat("COSINE SIMILARITY METRICS:\n")
  cat("Average similarity to diet strain:", round(strain_data$avg_sim_to_diet, 3), "\n")
  cat("Minimum similarity:", round(strain_data$min_sim_to_diet, 3), "\n")
  cat("Maximum similarity:", round(strain_data$max_sim_to_diet, 3), "\n")
  cat("Standard deviation (across samples):", round(strain_data$sd_sim_to_diet, 3), "\n")
  cat("Average within-sample SD:", round(strain_data$avg_within_sample_sd, 3), "\n\n")
  
  # Print sample-level results
  cat("SAMPLE-LEVEL RESULTS:\n")
  cat("Sample | Genes | Similarity | Within-Sample SD\n")
  cat(paste(rep("-", 60), collapse = ""), "\n")
  for (i in 1:nrow(strain_data$sample_results)) {
    cat(sprintf("%-15s | %6d | %10.3f | %16.3f\n",
               strain_data$sample_results$Sample[i],
               strain_data$sample_results$Genes[i],
               strain_data$sample_results$Sim_To_Diet[i],
               strain_data$sample_results$Within_Sample_SD[i]))
  }
  
  # Add chromosome-level results to the report
  if (!is.null(strain_data$chr_results) && nrow(strain_data$chr_results) > 0) {
    cat("\nCHROMOSOME-LEVEL RESULTS:\n")
    chr_summary <- strain_data$chr_results %>%
      group_by(chromosome) %>%
      summarize(
        avg_sim = mean(sim_diet, na.rm = TRUE),
        sd_sim_across_samples = sd(sim_diet, na.rm = TRUE),
        avg_within_sample_sd = mean(sim_diet_sd, na.rm = TRUE),
        avg_genes = mean(gene_count, na.rm = TRUE),
        .groups = 'drop'
      )
    
    cat("Chromosome | Avg Sim | SD (across samples) | Avg Within-Sample SD | Avg Genes\n")
    cat(paste(rep("-", 80), collapse = ""), "\n")
    for (i in 1:nrow(chr_summary)) {
      cat(sprintf("%-10s | %7.3f | %19.3f | %20.3f | %9.0f\n",
                 chr_summary$chromosome[i],
                 chr_summary$avg_sim[i],
                 chr_summary$sd_sim_across_samples[i],
                 chr_summary$avg_within_sample_sd[i],
                 chr_summary$avg_genes[i]))
    }
  }
  
  sink()
  
  cat("Report for strain", strain_cross, "generated at", report_file, "\n")
  
  # Also save the summary as an RDS file
  summary_file <- file.path(strain_cosine_dir, paste0(strain_cross, "_cosine_summary.rds"))
  saveRDS(strain_data, summary_file)
  
  # Save the raw results for this strain as well
  if (strain_cross %in% names(results)) {
    raw_results_file <- file.path(strain_cosine_dir, paste0(strain_cross, "_cosine_raw_results.rds"))
    saveRDS(results[[strain_cross]], raw_results_file)
  }
}

# Create overall summary file
overall_summary_file <- file.path(output_base_dir, "cc_diet_baseline_summary.txt")
sink(overall_summary_file)
cat("CC Diet Baseline Cosine Similarity Summary\n")
cat("============================================\n\n")
cat("Analysis Date:", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "\n\n")

# Create a comparison table
comparison_table <- data.frame(
  CC_Strain = character(),
  Diet_Strain = character(),
  Avg_Similarity = numeric(),
  Min_Similarity = numeric(),
  Max_Similarity = numeric(),
  SD_Across_Samples = numeric(),
  Avg_Within_Sample_SD = numeric(),
  Sample_Count = integer(),
  stringsAsFactors = FALSE
)

for (strain_cross in names(summary)) {
  strain_data <- summary[[strain_cross]]
  
  comparison_table <- rbind(comparison_table, data.frame(
    CC_Strain = strain_cross,
    Diet_Strain = strain_data$diet_strain_id,
    Avg_Similarity = round(strain_data$avg_sim_to_diet, 3),
    Min_Similarity = round(strain_data$min_sim_to_diet, 3),
    Max_Similarity = round(strain_data$max_sim_to_diet, 3),
    SD_Across_Samples = round(strain_data$sd_sim_to_diet, 3),
    Avg_Within_Sample_SD = round(strain_data$avg_within_sample_sd, 3),
    Sample_Count = strain_data$sample_count,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  ))
}

cat("COMPARISON OF DIET STRAINS:\n")
print(comparison_table)
sink()

cat("Overall summary generated at", overall_summary_file, "\n")

# Save overall results as RDS for future reference
saveRDS(summary, file.path(output_base_dir, "cc_diet_baseline_summary.rds"))
```

```{r}
# Using UNC 
# CC001: 0.947
# CC019: 0.909
# CC040: 0.934

# Using CCDiet
# CC001: 0.957 +- 0.008
# CC019: 0.924 +- 0.004
# CC040: 0.930 +- 0.009

# = better for 01 and 19, slightly worse for 40 
```

## 9. Generate CCRIX combined parental diplotype matrices

```{r}
# Updated function to generate sex-specific combined matrices for RIX combinations from CCDiet data
generate_ccrix_combined_matrices_by_sex <- function(diet_interpolated_probs, output_dir = "Data/CCDiet/CCDiet_Comparison/RIX/Combined_matrices") {
  # Make sure output directory exists
  if (!dir.exists(output_dir)) {
    dir.create(output_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  }
  
  # Define the RIX combinations using CCDiet strain IDs
  rix_combinations <- list(
    "RIX1" = c("CC-276", "CC-309"),    # Equivalent to CC001 x CC019
    "RIX2" = c("CC-309", "CC-284"),    # Equivalent to CC019 x CC040
    "RIX3" = c("CC-276", "CC-284")     # Equivalent to CC001 x CC040
  )
  
  # Function to create combined matrix for a chromosome, taking sex into account
  create_combined_chromosome <- function(chromosome_data, parent1_idx, parent2_idx, is_female, is_x_chr) {
    # Get the number of genes in this chromosome
    n_genes <- dim(chromosome_data)[3]
    
    # Create a matrix to store the combined probabilities
    combined_matrix <- array(0, dim = c(1, dim(chromosome_data)[2], n_genes))
    
    # Name the dimensions appropriately
    dimnames(combined_matrix) <- list(
      "Combined",
      dimnames(chromosome_data)[[2]], 
      dimnames(chromosome_data)[[3]]
    )
    
    # For each gene, combine parent probabilities based on sex and chromosome
    for (gene_idx in 1:n_genes) {
      parent1_probs <- chromosome_data[parent1_idx, , gene_idx]
      parent2_probs <- chromosome_data[parent2_idx, , gene_idx]
      
      # For X chromosome in males, only use maternal X
      if (!is_female && is_x_chr) {
        # For males, X chromosome is only from the mother (parent1 in forward cross)
        combined_matrix[1, , gene_idx] <- parent1_probs
      } else {
        # For autosomes (both sexes) and X in females, average both parents
        combined_matrix[1, , gene_idx] <- (parent1_probs + parent2_probs) / 2
      }
    }
    
    return(combined_matrix)
  }
  
  # Process each RIX combination for both forward and reverse crosses (to account for sex differences)
  for (rix_name in names(rix_combinations)) {
    # Forward cross (parent1 = female, parent2 = male)
    cat("Processing", rix_name, "Forward Cross...\n")
    
    parent1 <- rix_combinations[[rix_name]][1]  # mother
    parent2 <- rix_combinations[[rix_name]][2]  # father
    
    # Process both sexes for forward cross
    for (sex in c("Female", "Male")) {
      is_female <- sex == "Female"
      cross_direction <- "Forward"
      
      cat("  Creating", sex, "matrix for", cross_direction, "cross:", parent1, "(♀) ×", parent2, "(♂)\n")
      
      # Create a new interpolated_probs structure for the combined data
      combined_probs <- list()
      
      # Process each chromosome
      for (chr in names(diet_interpolated_probs)) {
        cat("    Processing chromosome", chr, "\n")
        
        # Determine if this is X chromosome
        is_x_chr <- chr == "X"
        
        # Find parent indices in this chromosome
        parent1_idx <- which(dimnames(diet_interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[1]] == parent1)
        parent2_idx <- which(dimnames(diet_interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[1]] == parent2)
        
        if (length(parent1_idx) == 0 || length(parent2_idx) == 0) {
          cat("    Warning: Could not find both parents in chromosome", chr, "\n")
          next
        }
        
        # Create combined matrix for this chromosome, accounting for sex and X chromosome
        combined_probs[[chr]] <- create_combined_chromosome(
          diet_interpolated_probs[[chr]], parent1_idx, parent2_idx, is_female, is_x_chr
        )
      }
      
      # Save the combined matrix
      output_file <- file.path(output_dir, paste0(rix_name, "_", cross_direction, "_", sex, "_combined_matrix.rds"))
      saveRDS(combined_probs, output_file)
      cat("  Saved combined matrix to", output_file, "\n")
    }
    
    # Reverse cross (parent2 = female, parent1 = male)
    cat("Processing", rix_name, "Reverse Cross...\n")
    
    parent1 <- rix_combinations[[rix_name]][2]  # mother in reverse cross
    parent2 <- rix_combinations[[rix_name]][1]  # father in reverse cross
    
    # Process both sexes for reverse cross
    for (sex in c("Female", "Male")) {
      is_female <- sex == "Female"
      cross_direction <- "Reverse"
      
      cat("  Creating", sex, "matrix for", cross_direction, "cross:", parent1, "(♀) ×", parent2, "(♂)\n")
      
      # Create a new interpolated_probs structure for the combined data
      combined_probs <- list()
      
      # Process each chromosome
      for (chr in names(diet_interpolated_probs)) {
        cat("    Processing chromosome", chr, "\n")
        
        # Determine if this is X chromosome
        is_x_chr <- chr == "X"
        
        # Find parent indices in this chromosome
        parent1_idx <- which(dimnames(diet_interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[1]] == parent1)
        parent2_idx <- which(dimnames(diet_interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[1]] == parent2)
        
        if (length(parent1_idx) == 0 || length(parent2_idx) == 0) {
          cat("    Warning: Could not find both parents in chromosome", chr, "\n")
          next
        }
        
        # Create combined matrix for this chromosome, accounting for sex and X chromosome
        combined_probs[[chr]] <- create_combined_chromosome(
          diet_interpolated_probs[[chr]], parent1_idx, parent2_idx, is_female, is_x_chr
        )
      }
      
      # Save the combined matrix
      output_file <- file.path(output_dir, paste0(rix_name, "_", cross_direction, "_", sex, "_combined_matrix.rds"))
      saveRDS(combined_probs, output_file)
      cat("  Saved combined matrix to", output_file, "\n")
    }
  }
  
  cat("All CCDiet RIX combined matrices generated successfully.\n")
  return(output_dir)
}

# Example usage:
generate_ccrix_combined_matrices_by_sex(CCDiet_gene_interpolated_probs)
```

## 10. Compare GBRS-derived CCRIX diplotypes vs "Combined matrices by Sex" from CCDiet_gene_interpolated_probs – Cosine similarity

```{r}
################################################################################
# CCDiet RIX Cosine Similarity Analysis - Using Combined Diet Matrices
# Updated to use CCDiet interpolated probabilities
################################################################################

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)

# ============================================================================
# SPECIFY YOUR INPUT FILES AND PARAMETERS HERE
# ============================================================================

# Input file paths - EDIT THESE PATHS to point to your data
metadata_file <- metadata_subset

ccrix_base_path <- "quantify_ASE_G0" 

interpolated_probs_file <- CCDiet_gene_interpolated_probs 

combined_matrices_dir <- "Data/CCDiet/CCDiet_Comparison/RIX/Combined_matrices"  

# Output settings
output_base_dir <- "Data/CCDiet/CCDiet_Comparison/RIX/Analysis"

# Optional: Specify a single cross to analyze (e.g., "CC001xCC019")
# Set to NULL to analyze all crosses in the metadata
target_cross <- NULL

# ============================================================================
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ============================================================================

# Fixed cosine similarity function with proper error handling
cos_sim = function(a, b) {
  v = rep(1L, ncol(a))
  numerator <- (a * b) %*% v
  denominator <- sqrt(a^2 %*% v * b^2 %*% v)
  
  # Handle division by zero
  result <- numerator / denominator
  
  # Replace NaN values with 0 (no similarity)
  result[is.nan(result)] <- 0
  
  return(mean(result, na.rm = TRUE))
}

# Enhanced cosine similarity that also returns similarity per gene
cos_sim_per_gene = function(a, b) {
  v = rep(1L, ncol(a))
  numerator <- (a * b) %*% v
  denominator <- sqrt(a^2 %*% v * b^2 %*% v)
  
  # Handle division by zero
  gene_sims <- numerator / denominator
  
  # Replace NaN values with 0 (no similarity)
  gene_sims[is.nan(gene_sims)] <- 0
  
  return(list(
    overall_sim = mean(gene_sims, na.rm = TRUE),
    per_gene_sim = gene_sims
  ))
}

# Convert CCRIX read counts to allele proportions matrix
ccrix_to_proportions <- function(ccrix_counts) {
  # Initialize the proportions matrix
  prop_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = nrow(ccrix_counts), ncol = 8)
  rownames(prop_matrix) <- ccrix_counts$locus
  colnames(prop_matrix) <- LETTERS[1:8]
  
  # Fill in proportions
  for (i in 1:nrow(ccrix_counts)) {
    if (!is.na(ccrix_counts$total[i]) && ccrix_counts$total[i] > 0) {
      prop_matrix[i, ] <- as.numeric(ccrix_counts[i, LETTERS[1:8]]) / ccrix_counts$total[i]
    }
  }
  
  return(prop_matrix)
}

# Extract probability matrices for a strain across all chromosomes
extract_strain_probs <- function(interpolated_probs, strain_name, gene_ids) {
  # Create matrix for this strain
  strain_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = length(gene_ids), ncol = 8)
  rownames(strain_matrix) <- gene_ids
  colnames(strain_matrix) <- LETTERS[1:8]
  
  # Find the strain's index in each chromosome's data
  for (chr in names(interpolated_probs)) {
    # Try exact match
    strain_idx <- which(dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[1]] == strain_name)
    
    if (length(strain_idx) > 0) {
      # Get genes for this chromosome
      chr_genes <- intersect(gene_ids, dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]])
      
      # Extract probabilities for this strain and these genes
      for (gene in chr_genes) {
        if (gene %in% dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]]) {
          gene_idx <- match(gene, dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]])
          strain_matrix[gene, ] <- interpolated_probs[[chr]][strain_idx[1], , gene_idx]
        }
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(strain_matrix)
}

# Extract probability matrix from combined parental matrix
extract_combined_probs <- function(combined_matrix, gene_ids) {
  # Create matrix for the combined reference
  combined_strain_matrix <- matrix(0, nrow = length(gene_ids), ncol = 8)
  rownames(combined_strain_matrix) <- gene_ids
  colnames(combined_strain_matrix) <- LETTERS[1:8]
  
  # Extract probabilities from the combined matrix
  for (chr in names(combined_matrix)) {
    # Get genes for this chromosome
    chr_genes <- intersect(gene_ids, dimnames(combined_matrix[[chr]])[[3]])
    
    # Extract probabilities for these genes
    for (gene in chr_genes) {
      if (gene %in% dimnames(combined_matrix[[chr]])[[3]]) {
        gene_idx <- match(gene, dimnames(combined_matrix[[chr]])[[3]])
        # Combined matrix has only one row ("Combined")
        combined_strain_matrix[gene, ] <- combined_matrix[[chr]][1, , gene_idx]
      }
    }
  }
  
  return(combined_strain_matrix)
}

# Modified CCRIX sample analysis using only combined parental matrix
analyze_sample_cosine <- function(sample_id, ccrix_base_path, interpolated_probs, 
                                combined_matrix, parent1, parent2) {
  # Construct file path
  sample_file <- file.path(ccrix_base_path, sample_id, 
                          paste0(sample_id, "_gbrs.quantified.diploid.genes.expected_read_counts"))
  
  # Check if file exists
  if (!file.exists(sample_file)) {
    cat("File not found for sample", sample_id, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Load CCRIX alignment counts
  ccrix_counts <- tryCatch({
    read.table(sample_file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  }, error = function(e) {
    cat("Error reading file for sample", sample_id, ":", e$message, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  })
  
  if (is.null(ccrix_counts) || nrow(ccrix_counts) == 0) {
    cat("Empty file for sample", sample_id, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Check required columns
  required_cols <- c("locus", "total", LETTERS[1:8])
  if (!all(required_cols %in% colnames(ccrix_counts))) {
    cat("Missing required columns for sample", sample_id, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Convert CCRIX data to proportions matrix
  ccrix_matrix <- ccrix_to_proportions(ccrix_counts)
  
  # Get gene IDs to analyze (those in the CCRIX file with non-zero totals)
  valid_genes <- ccrix_counts$locus[!is.na(ccrix_counts$total) & ccrix_counts$total > 0]
  
  # Extract combined matrix for these genes
  combined_parent_matrix <- extract_combined_probs(combined_matrix, valid_genes)
  
  # Ensure all matrices have the same rows (genes)
  common_genes <- intersect(rownames(ccrix_matrix), rownames(combined_parent_matrix))
  
  if (length(common_genes) == 0) {
    cat("No common genes found for sample", sample_id, "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Add diagnostic info
  cat("Sample", sample_id, "has", length(common_genes), "common genes\n")
  
  # Subset matrices to common genes
  ccrix_matrix <- ccrix_matrix[common_genes, ]
  combined_parent_matrix <- combined_parent_matrix[common_genes, ]
  
  # Remove rows that are all zeros in the ccrix matrix or combined matrix
  non_zero_rows <- rowSums(ccrix_matrix) > 0 & rowSums(combined_parent_matrix) > 0
  
  cat("Sample", sample_id, "has", sum(non_zero_rows), "non-zero rows out of", length(non_zero_rows), "\n")
  
  if (sum(non_zero_rows) == 0) {
    cat("ERROR: No valid non-zero rows found for comparison\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  ccrix_matrix <- ccrix_matrix[non_zero_rows, ]
  combined_parent_matrix <- combined_parent_matrix[non_zero_rows, ]
  
  # Calculate similarity to combined matrix
  sim_to_combined <- cos_sim(ccrix_matrix, combined_parent_matrix)
  
  # For completeness, we'll still extract parent matrices and calculate similarities
  # (even though we don't need to display them)
  valid_genes_filtered <- rownames(ccrix_matrix)
  parent1_matrix <- extract_strain_probs(interpolated_probs, parent1, valid_genes_filtered)
  parent2_matrix <- extract_strain_probs(interpolated_probs, parent2, valid_genes_filtered)
  
  # Calculate similarity to individual parents
  sim_to_parent1 <- cos_sim(ccrix_matrix, parent1_matrix)
  sim_to_parent2 <- cos_sim(ccrix_matrix, parent2_matrix)
  
  # Calculate similarity between parents (as reference)
  parent1_parent2_sim <- cos_sim(parent1_matrix, parent2_matrix)
  
  # Calculate similarity of combined matrix to each parent
  combined_to_parent1 <- cos_sim(combined_parent_matrix, parent1_matrix)
  combined_to_parent2 <- cos_sim(combined_parent_matrix, parent2_matrix)
  
  # Calculate per-gene similarities for detailed analysis
  detailed_sim_parent1 <- cos_sim_per_gene(ccrix_matrix, parent1_matrix)
  detailed_sim_parent2 <- cos_sim_per_gene(ccrix_matrix, parent2_matrix)
  detailed_sim_combined <- cos_sim_per_gene(ccrix_matrix, combined_parent_matrix)
  
  # Calculate gene-level similarities
  gene_similarities <- data.frame(
    gene = rownames(ccrix_matrix),
    sim_parent1 = detailed_sim_parent1$per_gene_sim,
    sim_parent2 = detailed_sim_parent2$per_gene_sim,
    sim_combined = detailed_sim_combined$per_gene_sim,
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  # Map genes to chromosomes
  chromosomes <- rep("unknown", length(gene_similarities$gene))
  names(chromosomes) <- gene_similarities$gene
  
  # For each chromosome in the interpolated_probs list
  for (chr in names(interpolated_probs)) {
    # Get genes in this chromosome from third dimension of array
    chr_genes <- dimnames(interpolated_probs[[chr]])[[3]]
    
    # Find matching genes between our gene set and this chromosome
    matching_genes <- intersect(gene_similarities$gene, chr_genes)
    
    # Set chromosome for these genes
    if (length(matching_genes) > 0) {
      chromosomes[matching_genes] <- paste0("chr", chr)
    }
  }
  
  # Add chromosome information to the gene_similarities dataframe
  gene_similarities$chromosome <- chromosomes[gene_similarities$gene]
  
  # Calculate per-chromosome similarity
  chr_similarities <- gene_similarities %>%
    dplyr::group_by(chromosome) %>%
    dplyr::summarize(
      sim_combined = mean(sim_combined, na.rm = TRUE),
      sim_parent1 = mean(sim_parent1, na.rm = TRUE),
      sim_parent2 = mean(sim_parent2, na.rm = TRUE),
      gene_count = dplyr::n()
    )
  
  # Create final result object
  result <- list(
    sample_id = sample_id,
    n_genes = nrow(ccrix_matrix),
    sim_to_parent1 = sim_to_parent1,
    sim_to_parent2 = sim_to_parent2,
    sim_to_combined = sim_to_combined,
    parent1_parent2_sim = parent1_parent2_sim,
    combined_to_parent1 = combined_to_parent1,
    combined_to_parent2 = combined_to_parent2,
    
    # Detailed results for further analysis
    gene_similarities = gene_similarities,
    chr_similarities = chr_similarities
  )
  
  return(result)
}

# Modified summarize function with simplified reporting
summarize_cosine_results <- function(cosine_results, parent_strains) {
  summary <- list()
  
  for (cross_type in names(cosine_results)) {
    # Skip if no samples for this cross
    if (length(cosine_results[[cross_type]]) == 0) {
      next
    }
    
    cat("\n=======================================\n")
    cat("Cross:", cross_type, "\n")
    
    # Get parents for this cross
    parents <- parent_strains[[cross_type]]
    parent1 <- parents[1]
    parent2 <- parents[2]
    
    cat("Parental strains:", paste(parents, collapse = " x "), "\n")
    
    # Collect statistics
    sample_count <- length(cosine_results[[cross_type]])
    cat("Total number of samples:", sample_count, "\n")
    
    # Group samples by sex for separate M/F summaries
    samples_by_sex <- list(
      "Female" = names(cosine_results[[cross_type]])[sapply(cosine_results[[cross_type]], function(x) x$sex == "Female")],
      "Male" = names(cosine_results[[cross_type]])[sapply(cosine_results[[cross_type]], function(x) x$sex == "Male")]
    )
    
    cat("Female samples:", length(samples_by_sex$Female), "\n")
    cat("Male samples:", length(samples_by_sex$Male), "\n\n")
    
    # Combine all samples for cross-level results
    all_samples_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(cosine_results[[cross_type]]), function(sample) {
      result <- cosine_results[[cross_type]][[sample]]
      df <- data.frame(
        Sample = sample,
        Sex = result$sex,
        RIX = result$rix,
        Direction = result$cross_direction,
        Matrix = result$matrix_key,
        Genes = result$n_genes,
        Sim_Combined = round(result$sim_to_combined, 3),
        Sim_Parent1 = round(result$sim_to_parent1, 3),
        Sim_Parent2 = round(result$sim_to_parent2, 3),
        stringsAsFactors = FALSE
      )
      return(df)
    }))
    
    # Cross-level summary
    cat("=== OVERALL CROSS SUMMARY ===\n")
    
    # Aggregate results for all samples
    sim_to_parent1 <- sapply(cosine_results[[cross_type]], function(x) x$sim_to_parent1)
    sim_to_parent2 <- sapply(cosine_results[[cross_type]], function(x) x$sim_to_parent2)
    sim_to_combined <- sapply(cosine_results[[cross_type]], function(x) x$sim_to_combined)
    
    # Get parent-parent and combined-parent similarities
    parent1_parent2_sim <- mean(sapply(cosine_results[[cross_type]], function(x) x$parent1_parent2_sim))
    combined_to_parent1 <- mean(sapply(cosine_results[[cross_type]], function(x) x$combined_to_parent1))
    combined_to_parent2 <- mean(sapply(cosine_results[[cross_type]], function(x) x$combined_to_parent2))
    
    genes_analyzed <- sapply(cosine_results[[cross_type]], function(x) x$n_genes)
    
    cat("Total genes analyzed:", sum(genes_analyzed), "\n")
    cat("Average genes per sample:", round(mean(genes_analyzed)), "\n\n")
    
    cat("COSINE SIMILARITY METRICS:\n")
    cat("Average similarity to combined reference:", round(mean(sim_to_combined), 3), "\n")
    cat("Average similarity to", parent1, ":", round(mean(sim_to_parent1), 3), "\n")
    cat("Average similarity to", parent2, ":", round(mean(sim_to_parent2), 3), "\n")
    
    cat("Similarity between", parent1, "and", parent2, ":", round(parent1_parent2_sim, 3), "\n")
    cat("Similarity of combined matrix to", parent1, ":", round(combined_to_parent1, 3), "\n")
    cat("Similarity of combined matrix to", parent2, ":", round(combined_to_parent2, 3), "\n\n")
    
    # Collect chromosome-level summaries
    all_chr_df <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(cosine_results[[cross_type]]), function(sample) {
      result <- cosine_results[[cross_type]][[sample]]
      if (!is.null(result$chr_similarities)) {
        df <- result$chr_similarities
        df$Sample <- sample
        return(df)
      } else {
        return(NULL)
      }
    }))
    
    # Aggregate chromosome-level results
    if (!is.null(all_chr_df) && nrow(all_chr_df) > 0) {
      chr_summary <- all_chr_df %>%
        dplyr::group_by(chromosome) %>%
        dplyr::summarize(
          avg_sim_combined = mean(sim_combined, na.rm = TRUE),
          sd_sim_combined = sd(sim_combined, na.rm = TRUE),
          avg_genes = mean(gene_count, na.rm = TRUE)
        )
      
      cat("\n=== CHROMOSOME-LEVEL RESULTS ===\n")
      print(chr_summary)
    }
    
    # Store full summary for this cross
    summary[[cross_type]] <- list(
      cross = cross_type,
      parents = parents,
      sample_count = sample_count,
      all_samples = all_samples_df,
      chromosome_summary = chr_summary,
      
      # Cross-level metrics
      avg_sim_combined = mean(sim_to_combined),
      avg_sim_parent1 = mean(sim_to_parent1),
      avg_sim_parent2 = mean(sim_to_parent2),
      parent1_parent2_sim = parent1_parent2_sim,
      combined_to_parent1 = combined_to_parent1,
      combined_to_parent2 = combined_to_parent2,
      total_genes = sum(genes_analyzed),
      avg_genes_per_sample = mean(genes_analyzed)
    )
  }
  
  return(summary)
}

# Modified visualization function to focus only on combined reference and add chromosome similarity
create_visualizations <- function(summary, output_base_dir) {
  viz_dirs <- list()
  
  for (cross_type in names(summary)) {
    cross_data <- summary[[cross_type]]
    parents <- cross_data$parents
    parent1 <- parents[1]
    parent2 <- parents[2]
    
    # Create cross-specific directory structure
    cross_dir <- file.path(output_base_dir, cross_type, "Cosine")
    if (!dir.exists(cross_dir)) {
      dir.create(cross_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    }
    
    # Store the visualization directory for this cross
    viz_dirs[[cross_type]] <- cross_dir
    
    # Create visualizations for all samples
    if (nrow(cross_data$all_samples) > 0) {
      plot_data <- cross_data$all_samples
      
      # 1. Sample-by-sample plot showing ONLY combined reference
      sample_data <- plot_data %>%
        dplyr::select(Sample, Sim_Combined) %>%
        dplyr::mutate(
          Sample = factor(Sample, levels = Sample[order(Sim_Combined, decreasing = TRUE)])
        )
      
      sample_plot_sim <- ggplot(sample_data, aes(x = Sample, y = Sim_Combined)) +
        geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "green", alpha = 0.7) +
        theme_minimal() +
        labs(
          title = paste("Cosine Similarity to Combined Reference -", cross_type),
          y = "Cosine Similarity",
          x = "Sample ID"
        ) +
        geom_hline(yintercept = cross_data$avg_sim_combined, 
                   color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
        theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1, vjust = 0.5))
      
      # Save the sample similarity plot
      sample_sim_file <- file.path(cross_dir, paste0(cross_type, "_sample_similarity.pdf"))
      ggsave(sample_sim_file, sample_plot_sim, width = 12, height = 6)
      
      # 2. Chromosome-level similarity plot
      if (!is.null(cross_data$chromosome_summary) && nrow(cross_data$chromosome_summary) > 0) {
        chr_summary <- cross_data$chromosome_summary
        
        # Sort chromosomes to ensure proper ordering (chr1, chr2, ..., chr19, chrX)
        chr_summary$chromosome <- factor(chr_summary$chromosome, 
                                        levels = c(paste0("chr", 1:19), "chrX"))
        
        chr_summary <- chr_summary %>% dplyr::arrange(chromosome)
        
        # Create chromosome plot
        chr_plot <- ggplot(chr_summary, aes(x = chromosome, y = avg_sim_combined)) +
          geom_bar(stat = "identity", fill = "darkgreen", alpha = 0.7) +
          geom_errorbar(aes(ymin = avg_sim_combined - sd_sim_combined, 
                           ymax = avg_sim_combined + sd_sim_combined), width = 0.2) +
          theme_minimal() +
          labs(
            title = paste("Cosine Similarity by Chromosome -", cross_type),
            y = "Similarity to Combined Reference",
            x = "Chromosome"
          ) +
          geom_hline(yintercept = cross_data$avg_sim_combined, 
                    color = "red", linetype = "dashed") +
          theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1))
        
        # Save the chromosome plot
        chr_plot_file <- file.path(cross_dir, paste0(cross_type, "_chromosome_similarities.pdf"))
        ggsave(chr_plot_file, chr_plot, width = 10, height = 6)
      }
    }
    
    cat("Visualizations for cross", cross_type, "created in directory:", cross_dir, "\n")
  }
  
  return(viz_dirs)
}

# Add this updated function to create the cross-to-RIX mapping for CCDiet strains
create_cross_to_rix_mapping <- function() {
  # Define the mapping between cross types and RIX for CCDiet
  cross_to_rix <- list()
  
  # RIX1 includes CC001xCC019 and CC019xCC001
  cross_to_rix[["CC001xCC019"]] <- list(rix = "RIX1", direction = "Forward")
  cross_to_rix[["CC019xCC001"]] <- list(rix = "RIX1", direction = "Reverse")
  
  # RIX2 includes CC019xCC040 and CC040xCC019
  cross_to_rix[["CC019xCC040"]] <- list(rix = "RIX2", direction = "Forward")
  cross_to_rix[["CC040xCC019"]] <- list(rix = "RIX2", direction = "Reverse")
  
  # RIX3 includes CC001xCC040 and CC040xCC001
  cross_to_rix[["CC001xCC040"]] <- list(rix = "RIX3", direction = "Forward")
  cross_to_rix[["CC040xCC001"]] <- list(rix = "RIX3", direction = "Reverse")
  
  return(cross_to_rix)
}

# Define the CCDiet parent strain mapping
create_ccdiet_parent_mapping <- function() {
  # Define the mapping between cross types and CCDiet strains
  ccdiet_parents <- list()
  
  # Map CC cross types to CCDiet strains
  ccdiet_parents[["CC001xCC019"]] <- c("CC-276", "CC-309")
  ccdiet_parents[["CC019xCC001"]] <- c("CC-309", "CC-276")
  ccdiet_parents[["CC019xCC040"]] <- c("CC-309", "CC-284")
  ccdiet_parents[["CC040xCC019"]] <- c("CC-284", "CC-309")
  ccdiet_parents[["CC001xCC040"]] <- c("CC-276", "CC-284")
  ccdiet_parents[["CC040xCC001"]] <- c("CC-284", "CC-276")
  
  return(ccdiet_parents)
}

# ============================================================================
# FUNCTION TO LOAD COMBINED MATRICES
# ============================================================================

load_combined_matrices <- function(combined_matrices_dir) {
  if (!dir.exists(combined_matrices_dir)) {
    stop("Combined matrices directory not found: ", combined_matrices_dir)
  }
  
  # List all RDS files in the directory
  matrix_files <- list.files(combined_matrices_dir, pattern = ".*_combined_matrix\\.rds$", full.names = TRUE)
  
  if (length(matrix_files) == 0) {
    stop("No combined matrix files found in directory: ", combined_matrices_dir)
  }
  
  cat("Found", length(matrix_files), "combined matrix files\n")
  
  # Load all matrices into a named list
  combined_matrices <- list()
  for (file_path in matrix_files) {
    # Extract the key (RIX_Direction_Sex) from the filename
    filename <- basename(file_path)
    key <- sub("_combined_matrix\\.rds$", "", filename)
    
    cat("Loading matrix:", key, "\n")
    
    # Load the matrix
    matrix_data <- readRDS(file_path)
    combined_matrices[[key]] <- matrix_data
  }
  
  return(combined_matrices)
}

# ============================================================================
# ANALYZE SAMPLES FUNCTION
# ============================================================================

# Function to analyze all samples using the combined matrices

analyze_samples_cosine <- function(
  sample_list,
  ccrix_base_path,
  metadata_subset,
  interpolated_probs,
  combined_matrices,
  parent_strains,
  cross_to_rix,
  target_cross = NULL
) {
  results <- list()
  
  # Initialize results list for each cross type
  for (cross_type in names(parent_strains)) {
    results[[cross_type]] <- list()
  }
  
  # Process each sample
  for (sample_id in sample_list) {
    # Find this sample in the metadata
    sample_row <- which(as.character(metadata_subset$Animal.ID) == sample_id)
    
    if (length(sample_row) == 0) {
      cat("Skipping sample", sample_id, "- not found in metadata\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Get cross type and sex from metadata
    cross_type <- metadata_subset$Cross[sample_row[1]]
    sex <- metadata_subset$Sex[sample_row[1]]
    
    # Skip if targeting a specific cross and this isn't it
    if (!is.null(target_cross) && target_cross != "" && cross_type != target_cross) {
      next
    }
    
    # Skip if cross type not in parent_strains
    if (!(cross_type %in% names(parent_strains))) {
      cat("Skipping sample", sample_id, "- cross type", cross_type, "not defined in parent_strains\n")
      next
    }
    
    # Get parent strains for this cross
    parents <- parent_strains[[cross_type]]
    parent1 <- parents[1]
    parent2 <- parents[2]
    
    # Get RIX and direction information
    rix_info <- cross_to_rix[[cross_type]]
    rix <- rix_info$rix
    direction <- rix_info$direction
    
    # Determine which combined matrix to use
    # Convert sex to proper case format (first letter uppercase, rest lowercase)
    formatted_sex <- paste0(toupper(substr(sex, 1, 1)), tolower(substr(sex, 2, nchar(sex))))
    matrix_key <- paste(rix, direction, formatted_sex, sep = "_")
    
    # Check if we have a combined matrix for this combination
    if (!(matrix_key %in% names(combined_matrices))) {
      cat("WARNING: No combined matrix found for", matrix_key, "\n")
      cat("Available matrices:", paste(names(combined_matrices), collapse = ", "), "\n")
      cat("Trying case-insensitive match...\n")
      
      # Try case-insensitive match
      matrix_names_lower <- tolower(names(combined_matrices))
      matrix_key_lower <- tolower(matrix_key)
      match_idx <- which(matrix_names_lower == matrix_key_lower)
      
      if (length(match_idx) > 0) {
        matrix_key <- names(combined_matrices)[match_idx[1]]
        cat("Found case-insensitive match:", matrix_key, "\n")
      } else {
        cat("Trying to find a matrix for just", rix, "and", direction, "...\n")
        
        # Try to find any matrix for this RIX and direction
        possible_keys <- grep(paste0("^", rix, "_", direction), names(combined_matrices), value = TRUE)
        
        if (length(possible_keys) > 0) {
          matrix_key <- possible_keys[1]
          cat("Using matrix:", matrix_key, "\n")
        } else {
          cat("ERROR: No suitable matrix found for", sample_id, "\n")
          next
        }
      }
    }
    
    cat("Processing sample", sample_id, "(Cross:", cross_type, ", Sex:", sex, ", RIX:", rix, ", Direction:", direction, ")\n")
    
    # Get the combined matrix for this sample
    combined_matrix <- combined_matrices[[matrix_key]]
    
    # Analyze this sample against the combined matrix
    sample_result <- analyze_sample_cosine(
      sample_id = sample_id,
      ccrix_base_path = ccrix_base_path,
      interpolated_probs = interpolated_probs,
      combined_matrix = combined_matrix,
      parent1 = parent1,
      parent2 = parent2
    )
    
    if (!is.null(sample_result)) {
      # Add metadata to the result
      sample_result$sex <- sex
      sample_result$rix <- rix
      sample_result$cross_direction <- direction
      sample_result$matrix_key <- matrix_key
      
      # Store the result
      results[[cross_type]][[sample_id]] <- sample_result
      
      cat("Analyzed", sample_result$n_genes, "genes for sample", sample_id, "\n")
      cat("Cosine similarity to combined reference:", round(sample_result$sim_to_combined, 3), "\n")
    }
  }
  
  return(results)
}

# ============================================================================
# ANALYZE CHROMOSOME SIMILARITIES FUNCTION
# ============================================================================

analyze_chromosome_similarities <- function(results, summary, output_base_dir) {
  for (cross_type in names(results)) {
    cross_samples <- results[[cross_type]]
    
    if (length(cross_samples) == 0) {
      next
    }
    
    # Create cross-specific directory structure
    cross_dir <- file.path(output_base_dir, cross_type, "Cosine")
    if (!dir.exists(cross_dir)) {
      dir.create(cross_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    }
    
    # Extract chromosome data from all samples
    all_chr_data <- do.call(rbind, lapply(names(cross_samples), function(sample_id) {
      sample_result <- cross_samples[[sample_id]]
      
      if (!is.null(sample_result$chr_similarities)) {
        chr_data <- sample_result$chr_similarities
        chr_data$sample_id <- sample_id
        return(chr_data)
      } else {
        return(NULL)
      }
    }))
    
    if (!is.null(all_chr_data) && nrow(all_chr_data) > 0) {
      # Summarize by chromosome
      chr_summary <- all_chr_data %>%
        dplyr::group_by(chromosome) %>%
        dplyr::summarize(
          avg_sim_combined = mean(sim_combined, na.rm = TRUE),
          sd_sim_combined = sd(sim_combined, na.rm = TRUE),
          avg_gene_count = mean(gene_count, na.rm = TRUE),
          sample_count = dplyr::n_distinct(sample_id)
        )
      
      # Save chromosome summary
      chr_summary_file <- file.path(cross_dir, paste0(cross_type, "_chromosome_summary.csv"))
      write.csv(chr_summary, chr_summary_file, row.names = FALSE)
      
      cat("Chromosome similarity summary for", cross_type, "saved to", chr_summary_file, "\n")
    } else {
      cat("No chromosome similarity data available for", cross_type, "\n")
    }
  }
}

# ============================================================================
# GENERATE REPORTS FUNCTION
# ============================================================================

generate_reports <- function(summary, output_base_dir) {
  # Generate summary reports for each cross
  for (cross_type in names(summary)) {
    # Create cross-specific directory for output
    cross_dir <- file.path(output_base_dir, cross_type, "Cosine")
    if (!dir.exists(cross_dir)) {
      dir.create(cross_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    }
    
    # Generate report for this cross
    cross_data <- summary[[cross_type]]
    report_file <- file.path(cross_dir, paste0(cross_type, "_cosine_analysis_report.txt"))
    
    sink(report_file)
    cat("CCDiet RIX Cosine Similarity Analysis for", cross_type, "\n")
    cat(paste(rep("=", 50), collapse = ""), "\n\n")
    cat("Analysis Date:", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "\n")
    
    # Write cross-specific information
    cat("\nCross Type:", cross_type, "\n")
    cat("Parent Strains:", paste(cross_data$parents, collapse = " x "), "\n")
    cat("Number of Samples:", cross_data$sample_count, "\n")
    cat("Total Genes Analyzed:", cross_data$total_genes, "\n")
    cat("Average Genes per Sample:", round(cross_data$avg_genes_per_sample), "\n\n")
    
    cat("COSINE SIMILARITY METRICS:\n")
    cat("Average similarity to combined reference:", round(cross_data$avg_sim_combined, 3), "\n")
    
    # Print sample-level results
    cat("\nSAMPLE-LEVEL RESULTS:\n")
    print(cross_data$all_samples[, c("Sample", "Sex", "Genes", "Sim_Combined")])
    
    # Print chromosome-level results if available
    if (!is.null(cross_data$chromosome_summary)) {
      cat("\nCHROMOSOME-LEVEL RESULTS:\n")
      print(cross_data$chromosome_summary)
    }
    
    sink()
    
    cat("Report for cross", cross_type, "generated at", report_file, "\n")
    
    # Also save the summary as an RDS file
    summary_file <- file.path(cross_dir, paste0(cross_type, "_cosine_summary.rds"))
    saveRDS(cross_data, summary_file)
  }
  
  # Create overall summary file
  overall_summary_file <- file.path(output_base_dir, "ccdiet_rix_cosine_summary.txt")
  sink(overall_summary_file)
  cat("CCDiet RIX Cosine Similarity Summary\n")
  cat("============================================\n\n")
  cat("Analysis Date:", format(Sys.time(), "%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S"), "\n\n")
  
  # Create a comparison table
  comparison_table <- data.frame(
    Cross = character(),
    Parents = character(),
    Samples = integer(),
    Avg_Similarity = numeric(),
    stringsAsFactors = FALSE
  )
  
  for (cross_type in names(summary)) {
    cross_data <- summary[[cross_type]]
    
    comparison_table <- rbind(comparison_table, data.frame(
      Cross = cross_type,
      Parents = paste(cross_data$parents, collapse = " x "),
      Samples = cross_data$sample_count,
      Avg_Similarity = round(cross_data$avg_sim_combined, 3),
      stringsAsFactors = FALSE
    ))
  }
  
  cat("COMPARISON OF CROSSES:\n")
  print(comparison_table)
  sink()
  
  cat("Overall summary generated at", overall_summary_file, "\n")
}

# ============================================================================
# RUN CCRIX ANALYSIS FUNCTION
# ============================================================================

run_ccrix_analysis <- function(
  metadata_file,                 # Metadata file path or object
  ccrix_base_path,               # Base path to CCRIX data
  interpolated_probs_file,       # RDS file with interpolated probabilities  
  combined_matrices_dir,         # Directory with combined matrices
  output_base_dir = "Data/CCDiet/CCDiet_Comparison/RIX/Analysis",  # Output directory
  target_cross = NULL            # Optional: Specific cross to analyze
) {
  cat("Loading required data files...\n")
  
  # 1. Load metadata if it's a file path, or use as is if it's already loaded
  if (is.character(metadata_file) && file.exists(metadata_file)) {
    metadata <- read.csv(metadata_file, stringsAsFactors = FALSE)
  } else if (is.character(metadata_file)) {
    stop("Metadata file not found: ", metadata_file)
  } else {
    # Assume it's already loaded as a data frame
    metadata <- metadata_file
  }
  
  # Check metadata has required columns
  required_cols <- c("Animal.ID", "Cross", "Sex")
  missing_cols <- setdiff(required_cols, colnames(metadata))
  if (length(missing_cols) > 0) {
    stop("Metadata is missing required columns: ", paste(missing_cols, collapse = ", "))
  }
  
  # 2. Load interpolated probabilities
  if (is.character(interpolated_probs_file) && file.exists(interpolated_probs_file)) {
    interpolated_probs <- readRDS(interpolated_probs_file)
  } else if (is.character(interpolated_probs_file) && file.exists(paste0(interpolated_probs_file, ".rds"))) {
    interpolated_probs <- readRDS(paste0(interpolated_probs_file, ".rds"))
  } else if (is.character(interpolated_probs_file)) {
    # Check if it's an object in the environment
    if (exists(interpolated_probs_file)) {
      interpolated_probs <- get(interpolated_probs_file)
    } else {
      stop("Interpolated probabilities file not found: ", interpolated_probs_file)
    }
  } else {
    # Assume it's already loaded
    interpolated_probs <- interpolated_probs_file
  }
  
  # 3. Load combined matrices from directory
  combined_matrices <- load_combined_matrices(combined_matrices_dir)
  
  # 4. Verify the CCRIX base path exists
  if (!dir.exists(ccrix_base_path)) {
    stop("CCRIX base path not found: ", ccrix_base_path)
  }
  
  # 5. Create parent_strains using CCDiet mapping
  parent_strains <- create_ccdiet_parent_mapping()
  
  # Get unique crosses
  crosses <- unique(metadata$Cross)
  
  # Use predefined cross_to_rix mapping
  cross_to_rix <- create_cross_to_rix_mapping()
  
  # Check if the requested cross is in our mapping
  if (!is.null(target_cross) && !(target_cross %in% names(cross_to_rix))) {
    stop("Target cross not found in cross_to_rix mapping: ", target_cross)
  }
  
  # 6. Generate sample list from metadata
  sample_list <- as.character(metadata$Animal.ID)
  
  # 7. Filter metadata if target_cross is specified
  if (!is.null(target_cross) && target_cross != "") {
    if (!(target_cross %in% crosses)) {
      stop("Target cross not found in metadata: ", target_cross)
    }
    
    metadata_subset <- metadata[metadata$Cross == target_cross, ]
    cat("Analyzing only cross:", target_cross, "\n")
    cat("Found", nrow(metadata_subset), "samples for this cross\n")
  } else {
    metadata_subset <- metadata
    cat("Analyzing all crosses in metadata\n")
    cat("Total samples:", nrow(metadata_subset), "\n")
  }
  
  # Create output directory if it doesn't exist
  if (!dir.exists(output_base_dir)) {
    dir.create(output_base_dir, recursive = TRUE)
    cat("Created output directory:", output_base_dir, "\n")
  }
  
  # Print summary of what we're going to analyze
  cat("\n==================================================\n")
  cat("CCDiet RIX Analysis Configuration:\n")
  cat("==================================================\n")
  cat("CCRIX data path:", ccrix_base_path, "\n")
  cat("Output directory:", output_base_dir, "\n")
  if (!is.null(target_cross) && target_cross != "") {
    cat("Target cross:", target_cross, "\n")
  } else {
    cat("Crosses to analyze:", paste(names(parent_strains), collapse = ", "), "\n")
  }
  cat("Total samples:", length(sample_list), "\n")
  cat("==================================================\n\n")
  
  # 8. Run the analysis
  cat("Starting CCDiet RIX analysis...\n")
  
  # Run the analysis
  results <- analyze_samples_cosine(
    sample_list = sample_list,
    ccrix_base_path = ccrix_base_path,
    metadata_subset = metadata_subset,
    interpolated_probs = interpolated_probs,
    combined_matrices = combined_matrices,
    parent_strains = parent_strains,
    cross_to_rix = cross_to_rix,
    target_cross = target_cross
  )
  
  # No results generated
  if (length(results) == 0) {
    cat("No results generated. Check your parameters and data.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Summarize results
  cat("\nSummarizing results...\n")
  summary <- summarize_cosine_results(results, parent_strains)
  
  # Create visualizations
  cat("\nCreating visualizations...\n")
  viz_dirs <- create_visualizations(summary, output_base_dir)
  
  # Analyze chromosome-specific similarities
  cat("\nAnalyzing chromosome-specific similarities...\n")
  analyze_chromosome_similarities(results, summary, output_base_dir)
  
  # Generate reports
  cat("\nGenerating reports...\n")
  generate_reports(summary, output_base_dir)
  
  # Save overall results
  if (!is.null(target_cross) && target_cross != "") {
    # When targeting a specific cross, only save results for that cross
    saveRDS(summary, file.path(output_base_dir, paste0(target_cross, "_ccdiet_rix_analysis_summary.rds")))
    saveRDS(results, file.path(output_base_dir, paste0(target_cross, "_ccdiet_rix_analysis_results.rds")))
    
    # Only save raw results for the target cross
    if (target_cross %in% names(results)) {
      cross_dir <- file.path(output_base_dir, target_cross, "Cosine")
      if (!dir.exists(cross_dir)) {
        dir.create(cross_dir, recursive = TRUE)
      }
      raw_results_file <- file.path(cross_dir, paste0(target_cross, "_cosine_raw_results.rds"))
      saveRDS(results[[target_cross]], raw_results_file)
      cat("Saved raw results for cross", target_cross, "to", raw_results_file, "\n")
    }
  } else {
    # When analyzing all crosses, save everything
    saveRDS(summary, file.path(output_base_dir, "ccdiet_rix_analysis_summary.rds"))
    saveRDS(results, file.path(output_base_dir, "ccdiet_rix_analysis_results.rds"))
    
    # Save cross-specific raw results files
    for (cross_type in names(results)) {
      # Only save non-empty results
      if (length(results[[cross_type]]) > 0) {
        cross_dir <- file.path(output_base_dir, cross_type, "Cosine")
        if (!dir.exists(cross_dir)) {
          dir.create(cross_dir, recursive = TRUE)
        }
        raw_results_file <- file.path(cross_dir, paste0(cross_type, "_cosine_raw_results.rds"))
        saveRDS(results[[cross_type]], raw_results_file)
        cat("Saved raw results for cross", cross_type, "to", raw_results_file, "\n")
      }
    }
  }
  
  # Final message
  cat("\n==================================================\n")
  if (!is.null(target_cross) && target_cross != "") {
    cat("Analysis for cross", target_cross, "completed successfully!\n")
  } else {
    cat("Analysis for all crosses completed successfully!\n")
  }
  cat("Output files are in:", output_base_dir, "\n")
  cat("==================================================\n")
  
  return(list(results = results, summary = summary))
}

# ============================================================================
# RUN THE ANALYSIS
# ============================================================================

# Run the analysis with the parameters defined at the top of the script
analysis_results <- run_ccrix_analysis(
  metadata_file = metadata_file,
  ccrix_base_path = ccrix_base_path,
  interpolated_probs_file = interpolated_probs_file,
  combined_matrices_dir = combined_matrices_dir,
  output_base_dir = output_base_dir,
  target_cross = target_cross
)

# Print completion message
cat("\nAnalysis completed successfully. Results are stored in:", output_base_dir, "\n")
```

```{r}
# Best values for UNC (G0)
# 01x19: 0.891
# 19x40: 0.889
# 01x40: 0.907

# CCDiet values (G0)
# 01x19: 0.905
# 19x40: 0.901
# 01x40: 0.908

# CCDiet genotypes are even better! 
```

## 11. Determine/visualize discordant genes

```{r}
# ============================================================================
# Discordant Gene Analysis for CC Data
# ============================================================================

library(dplyr)
library(ggplot2)
library(tidyr)
library(gridExtra)

# ============================================================================
# CONFIGURATION - MODIFY THESE PARAMETERS
# ============================================================================

# Base directory where CC analysis results are stored
results_base_dir <- "Data/CCDiet/CCDiet_Comparison/CC"

genes_with_id_path <- NULL  # Set to NULL if genes_with_id is already in the environment

# Similarity threshold below which a gene is considered discordant
similarity_threshold <- 0.7

# Output directory for discordant gene analysis
output_base_dir <- "Data/CCDiet/CCDiet_Comparison/CC/Discordant"

# Optional: Specify a single cross to analyze (e.g., "CC001xCC019")
# Set to NULL to analyze all crosses
target_cross <- NULL

# Bin size for chromosome position binning (in Mb)
bin_size_mb <- 5

# ============================================================================
# HELPER FUNCTIONS
# ============================================================================

# Function to load gene coordinates if not already in environment
load_gene_coordinates <- function(genes_with_id_path) {
  if (!is.null(genes_with_id_path) && file.exists(genes_with_id_path)) {
    return(readRDS(genes_with_id_path))
  } else if (exists("genes_with_id")) {
    return(get("genes_with_id"))
  } else {
    stop("Gene coordinates not found. Please provide a valid path or ensure 'genes_with_id' is loaded.")
  }
}

# Function to load CC analysis results
load_ccrix_results <- function(results_base_dir, target_cross = NULL) {
  # If target_cross is specified, load only that cross's results
  if (!is.null(target_cross)) {
    # Look directly in the cross-specific directory
    cross_dir <- file.path(results_base_dir, target_cross, "Cosine")
    results_file <- file.path(cross_dir, paste0(target_cross, "_cosine_raw_results.rds"))
    
    if (file.exists(results_file)) {
      cat("Loading results for cross", target_cross, "from", results_file, "\n")
      results <- list()
      results[[target_cross]] <- readRDS(results_file)
      return(results)
    } else {
      stop("Results file not found for cross: ", target_cross, " at path: ", results_file)
    }
  } else {
    # Load all crosses
    results <- list()
    cross_dirs <- list.dirs(results_base_dir, full.names = FALSE, recursive = FALSE)
    
    for (cross in cross_dirs) {
      if (grepl("^CC\\d+xCC\\d+$", cross)) {  # Only process directories that look like crosses
        cross_file <- file.path(results_base_dir, cross, "Cosine", paste0(cross, "_cosine_raw_results.rds"))
        if (file.exists(cross_file)) {
          cat("Loading results for cross", cross, "from", cross_file, "\n")
          results[[cross]] <- readRDS(cross_file)
        }
      }
    }
    
    if (length(results) > 0) {
      return(results)
    } else {
      stop("No CCRIX analysis results found in:", results_base_dir)
    }
  }
}

# Modified function to identify discordant genes for a sample - ONLY using sim_diet
identify_sample_discordant_genes <- function(sample_result, similarity_threshold) {
  if (is.null(sample_result) || is.null(sample_result$gene_similarities)) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Get gene similarities
  gene_data <- sample_result$gene_similarities
  
  # Check if sim_diet exists
  if ("sim_diet" %in% colnames(gene_data)) {
    discordant_genes <- gene_data %>%
      dplyr::filter(sim_diet < similarity_threshold) %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        sample_id = sample_result$sample_id
      )
  } else {
    cat("'sim_diet' not found in gene similarities data.\n")
    cat("Available columns:", paste(colnames(gene_data), collapse=", "), "\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  return(discordant_genes)
}

# Function to merge discordant genes with genomic coordinates
merge_discordant_with_coordinates <- function(discordant_genes, gene_coords) {
  # Standardize gene identifiers
  gene_coords$gene_id <- gene_coords$ensembl_gene_id
  
  # For each gene in discordant_genes, find its coordinates
  merged_data <- discordant_genes %>%
    dplyr::left_join(
      gene_coords %>% 
        dplyr::select(gene_id, chromosome_name, start_position, end_position),
      by = c("gene" = "gene_id")
    )
  
  # Calculate gene midpoint position in Mb
  merged_data <- merged_data %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      midpoint_mb = (start_position + end_position) / 2000000,  # Convert to Mb
      chromosome_name = as.character(chromosome_name)
    )
  
  return(merged_data)
}

# Modified function to calculate concordance percentage by bin
bin_chromosome_positions <- function(merged_data, all_genes_by_chr, bin_size_mb = 5) {
  # First, identify unique discordant genes and their positions
  unique_discordant_genes <- merged_data %>%
    dplyr::select(gene, chromosome_name, midpoint_mb) %>%
    dplyr::distinct()
  
  # Create bins for all chromosomes present in the data
  chromosomes <- unique(unique_discordant_genes$chromosome_name)
  
  # Create a list to store results for each chromosome
  binned_results <- list()
  
  for (chr in chromosomes) {
    # Get all genes for this chromosome from the reference
    chr_all_genes <- all_genes_by_chr[[chr]]
    if (is.null(chr_all_genes) || length(chr_all_genes) == 0) {
      next
    }
    
    # Get discordant genes for this chromosome
    chr_discordant_genes <- unique_discordant_genes %>%
      dplyr::filter(chromosome_name == chr)
    
    # Calculate min and max positions based on all genes in the chromosome
    min_pos <- floor(min(chr_all_genes$midpoint_mb) / bin_size_mb) * bin_size_mb
    max_pos <- ceiling(max(chr_all_genes$midpoint_mb) / bin_size_mb) * bin_size_mb
    
    # Create bins from min to max position
    bins <- seq(min_pos, max_pos, by = bin_size_mb)
    
    # Initialize bin data
    bin_data <- data.frame(
      chromosome_name = chr,
      position_bin = bins,
      position_bin_label = paste0(bins, "-", bins + bin_size_mb),
      total_gene_count = 0,
      discordant_count = 0,
      pct_concordance = 0
    )
    
    # Count total genes in each bin
    for (i in 1:length(bins)) {
      bin_start <- bins[i]
      bin_end <- bin_start + bin_size_mb
      
      # Count all genes in this bin
      bin_data$total_gene_count[i] <- sum(
        chr_all_genes$midpoint_mb >= bin_start & 
        chr_all_genes$midpoint_mb < bin_end
      )
      
      # Count discordant genes in this bin
      bin_data$discordant_count[i] <- sum(
        chr_discordant_genes$midpoint_mb >= bin_start & 
        chr_discordant_genes$midpoint_mb < bin_end
      )
    }
    
    # Calculate concordance percentage (avoiding division by zero)
    bin_data <- bin_data %>%
      dplyr::mutate(
        pct_concordance = ifelse(
          total_gene_count > 0,
          100 * (1 - (discordant_count / total_gene_count)),
          NA
        )
      )
    
    # Store results for this chromosome
    binned_results[[chr]] <- bin_data
  }
  
  # Combine all chromosome results
  binned_data <- do.call(rbind, binned_results)
  
  return(binned_data)
}

# Function to create chromosome position plots with concordance percentage
create_chromosome_concordance_plot <- function(binned_data, chromosome, title = NULL) {
  # Filter data for the specified chromosome
  chr_data <- binned_data %>%
    dplyr::filter(chromosome_name == chromosome) %>%
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(pct_concordance))  # Remove bins with no genes
  
  # If no data for this chromosome, return NULL
  if (nrow(chr_data) == 0) {
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Create plot
  plot_title <- ifelse(
    is.null(title),
    paste0("Gene Concordance Distribution - Chromosome ", chromosome),
    title
  )
  
  p <- ggplot(chr_data, aes(x = position_bin, y = pct_concordance)) +
    geom_line(color = "blue", size = 1) +
    geom_point(size = 2, color = "blue") +
    ylim(0, 100) +
    labs(
      title = plot_title,
      x = "Chromosome Position (Mb)",
      y = "% Concordance"
    ) +
    theme_minimal() +
    theme(
      plot.title = element_text(hjust = 0.5, face = "bold")
    )
  
  return(p)
}

# Function to analyze discordant genes across all chromosomes
analyze_all_chromosomes <- function(cross_data, gene_coords, similarity_threshold, bin_size_mb,
                                   output_dir) {
  # Create list to store all discordant genes
  all_discordant <- list()
  
  # Process each sample
  for (sample_id in names(cross_data)) {
    cat("Processing sample:", sample_id, "\n")
    sample_result <- cross_data[[sample_id]]
    
    # Identify discordant genes
    discordant_genes <- identify_sample_discordant_genes(sample_result, similarity_threshold)
    
    if (!is.null(discordant_genes) && nrow(discordant_genes) > 0) {
      all_discordant[[sample_id]] <- discordant_genes
    }
  }
  
  # Combine all discordant genes
  if (length(all_discordant) == 0) {
    cat("No discordant genes found for any sample.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  combined_discordant <- do.call(rbind, all_discordant)
  
  # Merge with genomic coordinates
  merged_data <- merge_discordant_with_coordinates(combined_discordant, gene_coords)
  
  # Save the complete merged data
  full_data_file <- file.path(output_dir, "all_discordant_genes_with_coords.csv")
  write.csv(merged_data, full_data_file, row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Saved all discordant genes with coordinates to:", full_data_file, "\n")
  
  # Filter out entries with missing chromosomes or positions
  valid_data <- merged_data %>% 
    dplyr::filter(!is.na(chromosome_name) & !is.na(midpoint_mb))
  
  if (nrow(valid_data) == 0) {
    cat("No valid chromosome coordinate data found for discordant genes.\n")
    return(NULL)
  }
  
  # Prepare all genes with chromosome positions (for denominator in concordance calculation)
  all_genes_by_chr <- gene_coords %>%
    dplyr::mutate(
      midpoint_mb = (start_position + end_position) / 2000000,  # Convert to Mb
      chromosome_name = as.character(chromosome_name)
    ) %>%
    split(.$chromosome_name)
  
  # Count unique discordant genes by chromosome
  chr_summary <- valid_data %>%
    dplyr::group_by(chromosome_name, gene) %>%
    dplyr::summarize(
      sample_count = n_distinct(sample_id),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::group_by(chromosome_name) %>%
    dplyr::summarize(
      discordant_count = n(),  # Count unique genes, not sample×gene combinations
      avg_sample_count = mean(sample_count, na.rm = TRUE),
      .groups = "drop"
    ) %>%
    dplyr::arrange(chromosome_name)
  
  # Save chromosome summary
  chr_summary_file <- file.path(output_dir, "chromosome_discordance_summary.csv")
  write.csv(chr_summary, chr_summary_file, row.names = FALSE)
  cat("Saved chromosome discordance summary to:", chr_summary_file, "\n")
  
  # Create binned data for visualization with concordance percentage
  binned_data <- bin_chromosome_positions(valid_data, all_genes_by_chr, bin_size_mb)
  
  # Save binned data
  binned_data_file <- file.path(output_dir, "binned_concordance_data.csv")
  write.csv(binned_data, binned_data_file, row.names = FALSE)
  
  # Create individual chromosome plots
  chromosomes <- unique(valid_data$chromosome_name)
  chromosomes <- sort(chromosomes)  # Sort chromosomes
  
  # Create a directory for chromosome plots
  chr_plots_dir <- file.path(output_dir, "Chromosome_Plots")
  if (!dir.exists(chr_plots_dir)) {
    dir.create(chr_plots_dir, recursive = TRUE)
  }
```
